review_text: So, the game isn't perfect - there are occasional weird physics glitches, a couple markers refused to clear even after they were definitively done, and the game doesn't give you many upgrades to enjoy.  Oh, and the Impossible Climb quest is *way* too easy to miss.  It is, however, a lot of fun.  The combat is simple but a decent challenge; the platforming has some pretty interesting complications; the quests are both interesting and fairly well sign-posted, with the one exception mentioned above.  I don't think it quite deserves all the praise I've seen, but it definitely is worth your time.

Ich kam für die Story, ich blieb wegen chronischer Orientierungslosigkeit (100% Komplettierung!) ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐  Wo fange ich an? Dieses Spiel hat mich absolut süchtig gemacht. Ich habe nicht nur die Story durchgespielt, sondern mir direkt alle Erfolge geholt. Mein Arzt sagt, mein Puls müsste sich bis nächste Woche wieder normalisiert haben.  Hier sind ein paar Dinge, die ihr vor dem Kauf wissen solltet:  Der Berg Qaf ist kein Streichelzoo: Ich habe mehr Zeit in der Luft verbracht als ein durchschnittlicher Lufthansa-Pilot. Die Plattform-Herausforderungen haben mir Reflexe beschert, von denen ich gar nicht wusste, dass mein alternder Körper dazu fähig ist.  Die Zeitschleifen-Mechanik: Großartig gelöst! Schade nur, dass ich die Zeit nicht zurückdrehen kann, um die 5 Stunden zurückzubekommen, die ich mit dem Suchen der allerletzten versteckten Truhe verbracht habe.  Das Kampfsystem: Flüssiger als frisch geschmolzene Butter. Wenn man erstmal die Kombos draufhat, fühlt man sich wie ein unaufhaltsamer Halbgott. Wenn man sie nicht draufhat, fühlt man sich wie ein Sandsack für die Gegner. (Spoiler: Ich war anfangs der Sandsack).  Die In-Game-Fotofunktion: Ein absoluter Segen für mein von Demenz bedrohtes Gamer-Gehirn. Ich habe so viele Fotos von blockierten Wegen gemacht, dass meine In-Game-Galerie aussieht wie die Foto-Mediathek eines paranoiden Detektivs.  Fazit: > Wer auf knackige Kämpfe, grandiose Metroidvania-Action und gelegentliche Wutausbrüche steht, bei denen die Nachbarn die Polizei rufen wollen, ist hier genau richtig. Sargon hat jetzt zwar die Krone gerettet, aber ich brauche erst mal Urlaub von der Zeitreise. 10/10 – Würde mein Gamepad wieder fast gegen die Wand werfen!

Llegué a Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown por recomendación de mi hermano y ya teniéndolo ojeado ya que es una de las sagas d emi infancia, la verdad es que me ha sorprendido muchísimo. No esperaba encontrarme una joyita de metroidvania, y menos viniendo de Ubisoft. El trabajo realizado por Ubisoft Montpellier es digno de reconocimiento, especialmente sabiendo todo lo que ha pasado con el estudio.  Lo he jugado en la máxima dificultad y sin ayudas de guiado, y en ningún momento se me ha hecho pesado o frustrante. Al contrario: la exploración, el parkour y algunos jefes se vuelven mucho más satisfactorios gracias al desafío que conllevan. El diseño del mapa está muy bien planteado, invita constantemente a volver sobre tus pasos y descubrir zonas mediante las habilidades que vas consiguiendo, algo fundamental en cualquier gran metroidvania.  Pero si algo me ha conquistado ha sido el combate. La mezcla de combos, habilidades y parry, dos de mis mecánicas favoritas en cualquier juego de acción, es simplemente divertidísima. Además, la variedad de enemigos y jefes hace que las peleas se mantengan frescas durante toda la aventura.  El DLC también me ha parecido muy entretenido y un gran complemento para quienes se quedan con ganas de más desafío.  En definitiva, se queda marcado para mí como un metroidvania súper recomendado. Tiene una exploración buena, un combate bien equilibrado, un gran diseño de niveles y, para los cazadores de logros, un 100% muy asequible y disfrutable de conseguir. Una auténtica joyita que merece mucho más reconocimiento del que recibió.
Excellent Metroidvania. Une pépite

If you read my reviews I often used a lot of hyperbole and tend to shower praise on games I really like. When writing this review I found myself describing this as the best metroidvania I've played and a crying shame it is not more widely recognised etc etc. The truth is when trying to approach this game and be more subjective I just often time found myself not being able to say anything but positives, it is an incredible return to form for Prince of Persia seeing us come back to the games roots, it has excellent gameplay with incredible 2d combat, fluid movement and amazing QoL and it had fantastic presentation, music and graphics with my only gripe being the story isn't incredible but I still found it very compelling.   Let's start with the obvious, Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown is a modern day classic and a sign that when Ubisoft tries, they can make wonderful games. The game explores lots of new themes not seen in a PoP whilst also paying respect to the previous games that came before, you don't even play as a prince in this game but more a defender of Persia, Sargon. He is a wonderful protagonist who's borders on arrogant but also kind and almost somewhat naive. The supporting characters are wonderful and I did enjoy the story.   Gameplay is king however and lost crown is one of the best playing in the franchise, we are back to 2d and we are now a proper metroidvania. You will unlock movement abilities which some are pretty standard, others are revolutionary. The map is absolutely incredible with some of the best level design I've ever seen and the QoL is fantastic, I love the map in this game and the fact you can tailor how much guidance you want is incredible. One negative is that the game clearly draws ideas from hollow knight with it's own version of benches, currency working the same as well as upgrades. It's derivative...but I preferred the execution of it in lost crown.   Combat, oh damn son this is so good. It's incredible, it's deep with lots of options and it makes every encounter feel tense but satisfying. I can't really elaborate but it's my favourite combat in a metroidvania. Boss fights are also incredible, they are tough but fantastic spectacles that are some of the genres best.   So, yes I love this game. It is an incredible achievement that goes to show when Ubi put a dev team on a game with some passion they can craft works of art. The last I heard Ubi closed the team down so any hopes of a sequel is dead. A crying shame and such disrespect to the devs who've shown that there is still an appetite for PoP. A fantastic game and easily the best in the genre.

El DLC no te añade tanto contenido, es mas lineal y te da unas 2.5 hrs mas, es mas plataformeo que otra cosa y no aporta taaaanto a la historia principal pero al menos es entretenido el movimiento.  El juego base tiene una esencia de metroidvania pura y dura, demasiadas habilidades que cuesta masterizar y todas y cada una de ellas e llevan a un combate increíble tanto contra jefes como contra enemigos normales. El parry es bueno, los movimientos especiales tienen animaciones increíbles y los enemigos no son taaaan repetitivos como en otros juegos del género. Ubisoft se la sacó y la puso en la mesa con esta entrega. Definitivamente vale la pena, aunque yo esperaría a una buena oferta si lo piensan cazar.

Overall this was a really solid game. In some sense, I felt that it recaptured the vibe of Dark Souls 1 which is pretty wild. Fun skills and bosses, amazing visuals and environments, decent puzzles and platforming. Definitely deserves its reputation as one of the greats of the genre. tl;dr One of the best metroidvanias of modern time. Perfectly balanced and lots of fun. The side content is much much harder than the base game so beware. Highly recommend overall.  Fantastic metroidvania. When I first saw the trailer at the game awards in 2024(?) I thought it looked pretty good, but didn't have high hopes since it's Ubisoft. I was dead wrong.  This sits up there with the best of modern times like Hollow Knight, Silksong, and Blasphemous. The movement is very snappy and fun, combat is versatile and has a good amount of different combos, and the platforming is perfect. The story isn't life changing, but I think it does a good job keeping the world interesting.  I played on the second hardest difficulty and think it was challenging, while not overbearing. Bosses usually took me less than 5 tries, so if you want more of a challenge, maybe try the hardest difficulty.  The game is perfectly balanced until you reach the side content. It's like walking through a park enjoying the serenity and then getting attacked by a pack of wolves all of a sudden. The main game would be harder than Silksong and Aeterna Noctis if the side content was required. I may try to complete all the challenges, but so far they've left me with two black eyes and stolen my lunch money.  There are some minor bugs, but nothing game breaking.

I waited for it to be on a hefty discount before buying it even though I was pretty sure I'd like it. Thirty bucks is just too much for this kind of game unless it's literally amazing. This one isn't NOT amazing I think it being an overused franchise, at least it seems to be, it kind of has a reputation of "well yeah, it better be good" which in todays market really shouldn't be taken for granted. 23.5小时全成就&ubi全挑战，开头先上总结：非常优秀的一款类银河战士恶魔城游戏，虽然存在一些小缺陷，但是瑕不掩瑜，总体体验良好。易用性设计非常优秀，是可以成为新人第一款银恶的程度。老银恶爱好者也非常推荐游玩体验。  1. 画面&美术&音乐：放大看的话建模还是很粗糙的，但是整体游玩过程中影响不大。美术风格简洁明快，场景辨识度高，各个区域特色分明，23小时玩下来眼睛是全程不累的。至于音乐，可能是没带耳机的缘故，感觉存在感不强，有部分boss战甚至干脆没有音乐（可能是BUG）。 2. 操作手感：战斗方面，光是默认难度设置的弹反判定窗口和闪避无敌帧就已经相当宽松了，只要了解敌人招式以后基本都能100%弹反（在没有其他因素影响的情况下），并且后撤步还有相当强悍的取消性能，能避免部分敌人读指令一套练到死，可能是设计缺陷但管他呢爽到就完事了；空战性能不错，利用挑空可以大大减少推图时的战损。整体战斗节奏偏快，需要熟练各个能力的运用才能规避大量战损，但每次运用能力的时候都非常丝滑，熟练以后更是无缝衔接各种操作。同理，推图中遇到的各种跳跳乐、解谜也需要玩家手脑并用，好好想想自己所拥有的能力都能做到哪些事情，这种思考+尝试+修正+达成的快感真的非常令人享受。 3. 地图设计：在我玩过的银恶里，本作的地图设计足以称得上优秀。除了上文提及的美术设计与辨识度的优点以外，本作的地图复杂度不低，但初见时往往能凭直觉收集掉大多数收集项，遇到暂时过不去的能力锁部分也能用非常方便的自带截图功能方便记忆。与此同时，反复跑起来的烦躁感并不强，虽然看似大而杂，实际跑图时却总是在享受丝滑操作的时候不知不觉就抵达目的地了。此外可以提前透露一下，主角所能获得的能力都在主线中，不存在打到通关没有二段跳的情况，遇到能力锁不妨先推进主线再来查漏补缺，后期更是能解锁非常便利的某项能力，方便玩家全地图四处查漏补缺。 4. 跳跳乐&解谜：这两部分的占比值得单开一栏来说明，首先，主线中的跳跳乐和解谜部分不少，但都不难，官方应该是有意不为难单纯想通关的玩家。一些困难甚至可以说相当难的跳跳乐和解谜都塞在了支线和挑战关卡里，虽然并不强制玩家通过，但还是得给各位像我一样有挑战精神的玩家敲下警钟：难，是真的非常非常难。不过如果你也和我一样是解谜跳跳乐的享受者，那么恭喜你，你会体验到非常多手心冒汗的跳跳乐与大脑飞速运转的解谜环节。 5. 战斗体验：推图时很多小怪其实还是蛮难对付的，主要难点在于多只同场，如果能拆分成多个1v1那么将相当轻松。当然，官方塞怪的同时当然也给了解决方法，本作中的技能基本全是高伤AOE并且带无敌，当机会出现时不要犹豫快速利用技能清理大量敌人或高危单位是推荐的战斗方法。BOSS战相当值得说道，大多数BOSS的设计都很不错，属于初见时会忍不住想「这能打？」并在几次尝试后逐渐得心应手，变为「原来还可以这样！」的节奏。但小部分BOSS以及他们的一些招式确实非常阴间，我自己打的时候感觉简直阴得没边，还好数值够高不打无伤过去了。可能是这部分招式还在「这能打？」的阶段，但我目前确实没有找到正确解法，只能暂定为是他真的阴间。 6. 收集体验&RPG系统：本作的核心装备系统是和空洞骑士类似的护符系统，每个护符有不同的占格大小，推图时能收集到扩大总容量的道具以及许多护符。不过可惜的是许多护符都放在了商店中，导致推图时大多数时候的奖励只是金币，玩家需要用金币去商店购买这些独特的护符。这个见仁见智吧，虽说少了探索时获取的收获感，但同时也能快速获得自己想要的护符。不过正如上文所说「打BOSS不知道招式如何处理不过还好数值够高」，原因正是这个护符系统中很明显地有些护符的强度不是一个量级的，基本要焊死在身上大幅优化游戏体验，而其他一些护符又如同鸡肋，并没有什么太大用处，使得我全程从头到尾基本上是强护符一旦获取后就再也不会摘下来，除了一些如磁铁显血之外的功能性护符可以偶尔拆换。但总体来说，地图的奖励确实有很大一部分是金币和文档，这个需要提前做好预期，我个人觉得探图探到了就很爽，倒是不在乎奖励给我什么。 7. DLC地图：DLC的大地图也同样值得单开一栏进行说明，首先，假如你是一名对RPG系统、装备收集、舔图奖励有强需求的玩家，那我不建议游玩DLC地图，因为DLC地图几乎没有收集项，也没有多少战斗场景，仅有3枚BOSS特攻护符和10余条剧情对话可以收集。但假如你是和我一样，单纯觉得把这个图的解谜和跳跳乐部分通过掉、把地图各个角落探干净就很爽，能够享受「游玩地图」这一行为的玩家，那我很推荐游玩这个DLC。DLC总体难度并不高，2小时就能打完，除了好玩的地图以外，还附赠了一个打起来体验不错的关底BOSS，建议综合评估适不适合自己再购买。（不过真有人忍住不买完全版获得经典王子皮肤吗）  最后想说，谁能想到十几年前拿着盗版光盘玩波斯王子五部曲的小登，如今竟然能在成为银恶爱好者后玩到波斯王子正版的银恶呢，看到那些熟悉的皮肤真的泪目了，希望这个IP能继续下去。

Overall a good game.  As a metroidvania, it's quite good, has a lot of nice parts, good movement and mechanics for exploration that most of the time feel good and interesting. Some nice puzzles, a decent story, and the combat is... goodish. Some pretty good parts, but also some not great parts mixed about. Overall it's definintely one of the better metroidvanias, but not making it to one of the greats or top levels, still well worth getting and playing for most that enjoy them at some point, even if you wait till it's on sale if you have some others still to play.  The game is, overall I think, possibly rushed or just a bit hit and miss. Some parts look and feel amazing, and clearly had a lot of effort, then you find moments where your character has no animations during a fight, or the camera janks off screen because you got too close to a wall or something. The core idea of the movement and combat mechanics are quite good. But then some areas have troublesome collisions or some jank or bug that causes problems or annoyance, or a boss fight might have bad collisions or some mechanicly questionable designs that just don't make it fun. There are mostly good exploration and movement puzzles and stuff, and overall more than not are good boss fights, some very good, but the polish, time to refine or iterate on ideas, or make sure bugs are ironed out... doesn't seem to be there. You can still play and finish the game, nothing gamebreaking in my case, but just be prepared to sometimes hit some jank, or to feel yourself questionoining why the boss works this way, or doesn't... given the combat mechanics of the game. For mid level games this is normal enough... but when many parts of this game are so nicely refined and great looking and feeling... it stands out all the more.  So some people probably didn't have the time, or were given too many tasks to finish, while others had enough time/manpower for their jobs, it overall got done, but a few parts got a bit rushed in areas.  So ya, not a perfect game, will have those moments that get frusterating, or feel off. But for the most part, a good game. Definintely had a lot of fun and with a decent play length of probably 20+ hours for many trying to semi completionist it. I'd advise against trying to 100% everything given a few of those bugs/issues/sticking points, but you can still enjoy most of it pretty well.  Ubisoft aspect is... annoying, their pop up for achievements or various things is huge, you can't turn it off even disabling on screen pop ups/notificaitons in the settings, but other than that wasn't much of an issue and didn't stop anything from working. So I'd rather not have it, but didn't really break anything, just a minor annoyance not worth skipping the game over like with some companies launchers/publisher software. I would recommend installing on an SSD though. You can play on an older disk drive, but expect some issues with hitches whenever a boss uses a skill the first time or a new audio or effect pops up for the first ten or so minutes after the game starts if you don't.

This might just be the single best Metroidvania game I have ever played, and I've only battled like 2 bosses. (Edit 6/4/26 - All bosses are good. Move-tech is amazing) Soundtrack is beyond phenomenal (Gareth Coker, the creator of the Ori soundtrack), the gameplay feels really responsive, controls are intuitive, and combat is very satisfying to play. There are modes to replay boss battles too, and I absolutely am gonna go back and do the two I've fought so far and go for hitless.  I can't speak for the speedrunners out there that might want to see if they can access game areas early with some cheeky mechanic exploits, but as a standard playthrough, this is an incredibly satisfying game.  It's honestly a real shame that Ubisoft decided that 1 million copies of the game sold in 1 year was "disappointing" and they "canceled the sequel." Get your head out of your ass Ubisoft.   This has been the first game I've played in years from Ubisoft where I can feel the AAA title they so desperately cling to. Maybe your sales wouldn't have been so bad if you didn't try to release it as an EGS/Uplay exclusive for 8 months.  I desperately want to see more of this game, and will purchase one at launch if they ever make it.

Absolutely magnificent game! The map is enormous and exploration is extremely rewarding. Having the camera system so you can screenshot where there's something you can't reach with your current abilities is a great touch. The pacing of acquiring abilities is perfect and spread out so that you are able to spend time mastering each one before a new one is presented. The bosses are so fun to fight. You will die multiple times fighting them, but then you get their pattern down and the dance feels amazing when you get it right. There were times I defeated a boss and immediately fought them again just to see the fight again. The story isn't cinematic but it's fun and has some twists.  Easily a 20+ hour game. If you take your time and explore, 30+ hours. If you want to 100% it, 40+ hours. The time on this account is not representative of the time I've played it since it wasn't available on Steam first.   Ubisoft Connect is the only negative thing I can say about this game. Otherwise, 9/10.

El mejor juego de Ubisoft que he jugado, y uno de sus mejores sin duda. No esperaba que me fuera a enganchar con un Metroidvania, siendo esta la primera vez que prueban con este género si no me equivoco. El sistema del mapa con las capturas de pantalla es una idea excelente que debería ser utilizada más. El combate es bastante complejo, necesitas aprender sus timing para poder dominar bien los combates. Los escenarios son muy cinematográficos con ese estilo 2.5D, sobre todo el del mar congelado donde me quedé observándolo en detalle por varios minutos. Las plataformas están geniales. Entre más habilidades desbloquees, explorar se hace cada vez más entretenido. Lo que más me fascinó son las cinemáticas en medio de los combates, en especial cuando le haces el bloqueo perfecto a un enemigo.  Lamentablemente, el juego no puede ser perfecto. Lo más flojo encuentro que es la banda sonora. Solo recuerdo como 2 canciones en momentos concretos, la mayoría del tiempo era algo que estaba por estar. Incluso hubo un momento en donde se me bugeo el audio por como una hora y no me di ni cuenta por lo irrelevante que es. Las hitboxes de ciertos enemigos y trampas con cuestionables. A veces sentía que me golpeaban con telequinesis. Tuve varios bugs dentro de la partida, creo que la primera vez que tengo tantos dentro de un Metroidvania. Desde bloques invisibles, trampas invisibles, hitboxes mayores, no poder saltar, el audio que se iba y cinématicas que no cargaron bien. No sé si atribuir todo esto al launcher de Bugisoft que es obligatorio usar, pero lo haré de todos modos.  En conclusión, este es un muy buen juego que estoy seguro que si tuviera una secuela y mejoraran lo malo, sería una obra maestra... Pero estamos hablando de Bugisoft, así que eso no pasará. Y no me refiero a corregir los errores, sino a sacar una segunda parte.

A fantastic platformer featuring engaging mechanics, a solid story, and more than enough content to keep completionists busy. The game's lack of mainstream popularity is likely due to minimal launch marketing and a relatively high price point for the platformer genre.  What I especially loved was the level design. Every time you unlock a new mechanic, it integrates seamlessly into the newly discovered areas, allowing you to backtrack and solve puzzles that previously blocked your path. Taking a difficult detour always yields a worthwhile reward, making tough puzzles and intense battles feel incredibly satisfying.  For combat, you have a standard attack, a charged attack, a parry, and two special combos. These can be customized and enhanced using upgradeable runes. The boss fights strike a perfect balance—they are challenging but accessible enough that I never felt tempted to rage-quit.  If you are a completionist, the game is packed with secrets, puzzles, and replayable boss battles. As someone who usually struggles with navigating platformer layouts, I really appreciated how detailed the map was; it kept me from ever feeling lost in such a massive world.  Overall, this is an excellent game, and it’s a shame the development team was moved to other projects instead of continuing to build on this formula. If you catch it on a discount, I highly recommend picking it up.

So. This game is great, but man is Ubisoft annoying as heck. You HAVE TO sign for an Ubisoft account to play this game. On that fact alone, I'd say consider "other options" than buying this game.   The game itself is glorious. Fantastic controls and amazing QoL features along with a very sleek presentation. It's a no brainer if you like Metroidvanias or even just good 2D platformer/action hack and slash games in general.  EDIT after beating the game:  As the title says, Ubisoft claimed that the game didn't perform well enough with sales and they originally disbanded the team. The good news however, and it's very good news because this game goes hard, is that the team recently was reunited and we MAY see future PoP from them in the future.  What I loved about this game:  -The style. The game is a refreshing break from ultra-4k-Unreal-Engine-5-hope-you-have-a-really-expensive-graphics card presentation. It runs well on even a Switch 1 and has a style I found reminiscent of Saturday morning cartoons. The music is orchestral and beautifully captures the majesty of the deep middle eastern lore. The story also has a solid Saturday morning cartoon feel. It's no Expedition 33, but the voice acting is solid and the story is well told.  -PLATFORMING!!! Holy crap I love the platforming in this game. The controls of the prince are so tight it feels like there's barely a physical difference between your brain and what he's doing on screen. It's pretty much up there with Super Meat Boy and Celeste for me, although for the most part it never gets as frustrating as those titles unless you specifically do some of the game's optional challenges.  -Combat. It's SO fast and so fluid. When I fought some of these bosses I felt like I was playing an episode of Dragon Ball Z, yet it never felt too chaotic that I lost track of where I was.  -Mechanics. Lots of really clever design here. For people who hate the excessive backtracking in metroidvanias, this game lets you take snapshots in game on the map so you can see the power-up you will need and revisit it when you have it rather than trying to memorize stuff and backtracking only to find you have to come back later.  -Solid map. It's huge, but never tiresome. You get reasonably placed warp points, but not so many that world exploration is negated. They're very well spaced out. Late near the end game you do get the ability to travel between every one of the numerous save points so if you want to collect everything or finish up side quests, it's very fast and easy to do. This game really respects your time.  I honestly cannot think of any major criticisms for this game. If you like metroidvanias, this is pretty much a perfect formula of that genre.  I will say that the boss fights could be harder. On Normal, I beat all of them including the final boss in 1 or 2 tries. But note that this is partially because I was huge on exploring and maxing out resources in addition to using the amulet system to craft a really solid melee build. The game rewards you for engaging with its mechanics and exploration which I love.  If you want more of a challenge, toggle the difficulty to hard and do the optional platforming challenges. Personally, I prefer my metroidvanias to be more like comfort games rather than a soulslike game.  That's it. Just really wanna see more people play this game and get it the love it deserves. I got it on sale for 12 bucks and enjoyed every one of the 30 hours I played.

簡單總結：類銀河戰士惡魔城/橫向卷軸冒險，如果你喜歡空洞騎士的話可以玩得愉快的一款遊戲，難度可以自由調整所以不用怕自己過不了關/太簡單  之前特價的時候買的，基本上是類銀河戰士惡魔城那樣橫向卷軸的遊戲，慢慢探索+收集資源跟能力，雖然沒有像空洞騎士這樣便宜又大碗，但是整體的體驗很好；首先稱讚地圖，地圖範圍大，一開始經過某些地方就會幫你標示某些門要另一邊打開，除了可以放圖釘還有內建拍照標示的系統，回頭看地圖時方便很多，個人認為甚至比空洞騎士的地圖做的還好；傳送的話前期會解鎖，不過因為地圖大有點遠，中期的時候可以解鎖更多傳送點還不錯；打擊手感很不錯，戰鬥不是無腦砍需要稍微抓一下節奏；徽章系統也豐富，可以玩出很多不同的東西，但是要升級徽章我是覺得挺麻煩；跑酷的地方也很多很有趣，喜歡跑酷的話裡面肯定有不少等著你；也有需要解謎的地方，有些地方算是有點燒腦；水晶資源(道具升級資源)的話裡面打怪+路上獎勵給不少，不要亂花水晶跟決定好自己打算玩的走向一路上是不會缺水晶；整體來說表現不錯，是一款買了不會後悔遊戲(不過價格真的不便宜，Ubi很常特價就等一下吧~)

# Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown – Ein richtig gutes Spiel, das sich selbst irgendwann hasst  Früher war Prince of Persia: Warrior Within düster, edgy und ein 3D-Action-Adventure mit Wandläufen, Zeitkräften und diesem ganz speziellen Ubisoft-Charme aus den frühen 2000ern. Dann kam Prince of Persia: The Two Thrones und schloss die Trilogie halbwegs würdig ab.  Und jetzt? Jetzt ist Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown plötzlich ein 2D-Metroidvania. Der Prinz selbst spielt quasi keine Rolle mehr, stattdessen steuert man einen legendären Krieger. Klingt erstmal wie eine Ubisoft-typische „Wir benutzen die Marke einfach weiter“-Idee — und überraschenderweise funktioniert das anfangs sogar verdammt gut.  Denn gameplaytechnisch hat The Lost Crown richtig was drauf.  Das Kampfsystem ist wahrscheinlich das Beste, was Ubisoft seit Jahren gebaut hat. Angriffe, Ausweichmanöver und Parries haben genau die richtigen Timings, sodass Kämpfe angenehm aggressiv und gleichzeitig präzise wirken. Jeder perfekte Konter fühlt sich belohnend an, jede erfolgreiche Kombo befriedigend. Vor allem weil die Gegner später anfangen, selbst auszuweichen, Angriffe zu kontern oder einen komplett auseinanderzunehmen, wenn man einfach nur Button-Mashing betreibt.  Und tatsächlich: Die Tutorials sind hervorragend. Nicht dieses übliche „Drück X zum Gewinnen“, sondern wirklich sinnvolle Kampflektionen, die einem die Kombos, Mechaniken und Bewegungsoptionen vernünftig beibringen. Das merkt man später enorm, weil das Spiel durchaus anspruchsvoll wird und die Fähigkeiten auch aktiv eingefordert werden.  Auch die klassischen Prince-of-Persia-Elemente wurden übernommen: Fallen, Plattforming und Parkour-Sequenzen sind überall präsent. Teilweise erinnert das Ganze fast an die Escape-Passagen aus Ori and the Blind Forest oder Ori and the Will of the Wisps, wo man nach Bosskämpfen perfekte Bewegungsabläufe hinlegen musste, während hinter einem die Welt explodiert.  Der Unterschied ist nur: Ori wollte herausfordernd sein. The Lost Crown will irgendwann einfach persönlich beleidigend werden.  Gerade die Parkour-Challenges drehen später komplett frei. Die Fehlertoleranz liegt teilweise im Nanometerbereich, viele Passagen setzen blindes Springen voraus oder verlangen, dass man die komplette Sequenz bereits auswendig kennt. Nicht weil die Mechaniken schlecht wären — die Steuerung funktioniert hervorragend — sondern weil die Entwickler scheinbar beschlossen haben, dass „Trial and Error“ dieselbe Bedeutung wie „gutes Gamedesign“ hat.  Besonders schlimm wird es in einer optionalen Fallenkammer, die vermutlich unter dem Einfluss harter Betäubungsmittel entworfen wurde. Dort darf man gefühlt fünfzehn perfekte Bewegungsabläufe ohne Fehler hintereinander absolvieren, nur damit am Ende irgendein völlig belangloser Skin wartet. Keine interessante Belohnung. Kein cooles Lore-Element. Nur pure Spielerverachtung.  Und genau da kippt das Spiel langsam von „fordernd“ zu „anstrengend“.  Das Problem: Die eigentlichen Spielmechaniken sind so intensiv, dass man irgendwann regelrecht ermüdet. Ich hatte selten ein Spiel, das mich nach zwei Stunden körperlich und mental so ausgelaugt hat. Nicht wegen Horror, Story oder Spannung — sondern weil das permanente Hochkonzentrieren irgendwann einfach nervt. Das ist keine gute Art von Schwierigkeit mehr.  Dazu kommt das typische Metroidvania-Backtracking. Neue Fähigkeiten öffnen neue Wege, versteckte Kisten und Secrets warten überall — immerhin erlaubt das Spiel, interessante Stellen direkt auf der Karte zu markieren. Das ist tatsächlich extrem hilfreich und wahrscheinlich eine der besten Komfortfunktionen im gesamten Genre.  Nur bringt das wenig, wenn das Spiel einem gleichzeitig nie vernünftig sagt, wo man überhaupt hin soll.  Die Welt ist groß, verwinkelt und hübsch aufgebaut, aber man rennt oft völlig planlos durch neue Gebiete. Selbst gekaufte Karten bringen nur bedingt etwas, weil sie zwar Übersicht schaffen, aber keine klare Richtung vorgeben. Dazu kommen Teleporter, die viel zu selten verteilt sind. Pro Gebiet maximal ein paar Stück — und natürlich immer gerade weit genug auseinander, dass jeder Weg unnötig Zeit frisst.  Man merkt irgendwann richtig, wie das Spiel künstlich Länge erzeugt.  Storytechnisch bleibt ebenfalls wenig hängen. Die Handlung erfüllt ihren Zweck als Motivation fürs Gameplay, mehr aber auch nicht. Dialoge und Texte versuchen zwar Tiefe aufzubauen, schaffen es aber nie, eine wirkliche emotionale Bindung herzustellen. Auch die sammelbaren Lore-Texte und Artefakte sind erschreckend uninteressant geschrieben. Normalerweise lese ich so etwas gern, hier war mir irgendwann komplett egal, wer warum welches magische Artefakt vor 300 Jahren verloren hat.  Und dann gibt es noch Dinge, die einfach unnötig peinlich sind. Zum Beispiel: kein Ultra-Wide-Support. Schwarze Balken links und rechts im Jahr 2024 bei einem modernen 2D-Spiel. Großartig.  Dabei steckt unter all dem Frust wirklich ein fantastisches Spiel. Das Kampfsystem funktioniert hervorragend, die Bosskämpfe sind stark und das grundlegende Gameplay macht Spaß. Sehr viel sogar. Aber je länger The Lost Crown dauert, desto mehr sabotiert es sich selbst mit unnötig nervigem Design, überzogenem Plattforming und künstlicher Streckung.  Am Ende versteht man leider ziemlich gut, warum das Spiel trotz seiner Qualitäten nicht wirklich durch die Decke ging. Es ist kein schlechtes Spiel — im Gegenteil. Es ist wahrscheinlich eines der besten modernen Ubisoft-Spiele überhaupt.  Aber es fühlt sich gleichzeitig an, als hätte irgendein Teil des Entwicklerteams aktiv versucht, den Spieler irgendwann zu brechen.  Und genau das hinterlässt am Ende diesen bitteren Eindruck von verschwendetem Potenzial. Früher war Prince of Persia eine einzigartige Reihe voller cleverer Rätsel, stylischem Parkour und Abenteuergefühl. The Lost Crown zeigt kurz, dass diese Magie noch irgendwo existiert — nur um sie dann unter Frust, Backtracking und Designer-Ego langsam zu begraben.  Schade eigentlich. Wirklich schade.

Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown is worth every coin. The gameplay is sharp, the environments breathe with a sense of myth and place, and the characters carry genuine weight — this is a Persia that feels lived-in, not just dressed up.  What surprised me most was how deeply the story lands. Sargon's journey isn't just a backdrop — you feel what he's going through at every step. And Vahram? His arc is the kind that quietly stays with you. The lore doesn't just reference Persian mythology; it feels rooted in it.  But the epilogue — that's where this game earned a permanent place in my memory. It gives full justice to everything that came before it. The kind of ending that makes you sit still for a moment after the credits roll. Easily one of the best endings I've experienced in gaming.  Don't sleep on this one.

One of my favorite DOS games back in the 80s/90s was Prince of Persia.  I also enjoyed Sands of Time on the original XBox, but with the move to 3d it felt like a much different game.  I never did play the other 3D PoP games, though I picked them up during a Steam sale.    Since I've been on a bit of a Metroidvania kick lately and I saw this was a pretty well reviewed Metroidvania I had to pick it up.  I'm glad it did.  So far it's been a fun return to the franchise and feels a bit more like those original games.  The exploration and platforming has been fun so far.  The boss fights haven't been quite on the level of Hollowknight, but have still been fun.  I'm really liking the "memory" feature that lets you take a screenshot of an area that you can then view on the map.  It serves as a quick reminder when you find someplace you can't go yet.  You can just check the screenshot later to remind yourself why you couldn't go that way and quickly decide if you have now learned any abilities that will assist.  It also runs very well on the Steamdeck and my Linux desktop.  There is a bit of weirdness in the initial startup on the desktop when it is firing up Ubisoft connect, and I seem to have to manually quick and stop it once the game has closed, but the game itself has run flawlessly so far.

It's a pretty good and polished metroidvania, not all the bugs were ironed out though, a couple annoying ones persist, you can get stuck in some places. Each ability you get is utilized to their potential, albeit the early game is far harder than the late game.  The upgrades you get make you a bit too strong for the basic content, the difficulty balancing could be better. On the other hand the optional challenges offer the most difficulty and the best bosses, it's only sad there's no EX variant for all the bosses, because they were incredibly fun to fight compared to the basic variants.  The movement is really good and there are a few abilities in this game that are unique compared to other metroidvanias, but most other abilities are basic. I usually dislike desert areas in games, but this one had really cool visuals and areas. The combat with basic enemies kinda gets stale near the end though, and the final ability you get trivializes most encounters.  Overall, a pretty big surprise coming from Ubisoft, surprisingly good. Not my favorite or anything, but good.

Un des meilleurs platformers jamais créé. Et je pèse mes mots. C'est le meilleur jeu de mon SteamDeck sur lequel il fonctionne comme un charme.  Le jeu devient rapidement exigeant (sans parler des défis optionnels de l'enfer, heureusement il n'y a aucunes nécessité de les compléter, à réserver pour ceux qui aime la souffrance) il n'est donc pas à mettre dans toutes les mains.  Mais si vous aimer les combos créatifs, les combats vous raviront. Si vous aimer les énigmes, les niveaux puzzles vous tritureront l'esprit. Enfin le jeux est beau, une joli DA et des animations fluides et bien conçues.  Au risque de me répéter : C'est un must-have pour tout les possesseurs de SteamDeck ou équivalent.

平心而论，游戏是好游戏，算是上乘之作，但是我依然不打算给好评，给差评和游戏质量无关，完全是一些地方不喜欢又或是觉得恶心。  ·跳跳乐懂的都懂，也不是说多难吧，就是一段一段做的太长没站脚的地方又没容错，失误了极其浪费时间，还有这个传送回去的位置，在地图的一些地方很迷惑，为什么不是传送回第一次起跳的地方，而是更后面，感觉制作组很是痴迷于跳跳乐，我甚至觉得各个能力不是基于战斗，而是基于跳跳乐做的。  ·解密也是，有的解密不看攻略自己想是真的费时间，有的要自己一点一点试，我真的不想在这上面浪费那么多时间，但是你又不能不做，这个不像跳跳乐有跳过。  ·最后就是这游戏无论是小兵还是BOSS，性能和机动性应该是我玩过的游戏里最强的了，平A3第三段出手就后撤步，这情况太多太多了，我就在想，你做个+4，5段平A的护符意义在哪里？ 后面有些BOSS偏演出的，TMD瞬移来瞬移去半天就只能摸几下，技能也是不敢放，太容易空了，打不死军团那个射箭的BOSS，真的给我打笑了，离得远无限射箭，靠近立马瞬移走。  能接受这些那剩下的都是优点。

I didn’t expect this game to have such great mechanics and such a huge map to explore!  The only downside, in my opinion, was the boss fights on standard difficulty. They felt quite easy and relied too much on the parry mechanics in the early game, which I honestly didn’t enjoy that much. Later in the game, the bosses relied less on parrying, but they also became too easy because of the upgrades you unlock. I’m not sure how it feels on other difficulties, but my main issue was the scaling. It wasn’t balanced very well, although it wasn’t drastically bad either.  Overall, I really enjoyed it, and I would absolutely recommend it 10/10!

玩的时候当时看到了很多恶评，且我玩过的横板过关类游戏实在不算丰富，24年第一次上手后不久就弃了：操作不顺手，爽感不够高。今年把生活里的一些事情忙完以后又重新拿起来玩，突然发现这款游戏有自己独特的魅力。丰富的地图、丰富的能力与武器交互、连招与符文都能展现出开发者的想象力。很多设计的细节、流畅的交互、过场又无一不展现出开发者强大的功底。我奔着全收集去，现在进度96.79，还有一些实在不想磨时间的关卡没做。以后或许还会拿起来，把dlc打完。 讲的故事不复杂，但是的确有一些悬念和反转，算得上扣人心弦。因此从哪个角度来看，我都认为这部作品算得上优作。建议游玩，但不建议反复游玩。

Öncelikle söylemem gerekir ki beklediğimden kat ve kat daha uzun sürdü. Dolu dolu bir içeriği var hakkını vermek gerek. Beklediğim şeyler yaratıcı zorlayıcı parkurlardı beklediğimi aldım kesinlikle. Bunun dışında combatı bekledğimden daha az çeşitliydi oyunun başından sonuna baktığımızda çok değişmedi ancak var olan hareketlerin animasyonları ve vuruşun hissiyatı gerçekten çok iyi 2.5d bi oyun sonuçta o konuda şaşırdım. Hikayesi öyle aman aman bi hikaye değil ancak bazı sahneleri insana haa vay be dedirten cinsten. Gelelim boss fightlarına hepsini kestiğimi söylemeliyim 3 tane net aklımda kalan var o açıdan bence bazı bosslar hariç gerçekten düşünülmüş emek verilmiş çok çeşitli vuruş animasyonlarına sahip kesinlikle zorlayıcılardı. Son olarak puzzlelara da değineyim çok nadir çok zor vardı ama hepsi eğlenceliydi.  Genel kanım bu oyunun underrated olduğu gerçekten ubisoft mu yapmış diyosunuz çünkü şirketin hali ortada. Oyuna bi puan vericek olursam 84 derim. Gereksiz yere aynı yeri dolaşmaktan , bence daha iyi olabilicek grafiklerden çünkü yüze iyice zoom atınca çok komik duruyo ve biraz combat çeşitliliğinden kırdım.

Prince of Persia The Lost Crown is a seriously overlooked metroidvania that doesn't quite stand toe to toe with the giants of the genre, but it is not far behind.  The game isn't perfect, the story and writing are pretty average, and I find the artstyle and graphics a little bland, but the combat and platforming are fantastic and the level design is great.  This game is also much more accessible than a lot of metroidvanias because of its difficulty sliders and the memory mechanic which lets you essentially attach screenshots to you map. Granted I did not find the memory mechanic as useful as I thought I would, mainly because it is a limited resource so I ended up hoarding them despite the fact that you do get 20 or so total, which is plenty.  If you enjoy metroidvanias or are looking to get into them, I highly recommend this one.

Not my thing. I was not sure with the demo when I played it, but thought it was just the demo effect, and it's better to try the whole game. Good thing, that I got this one at a discount, I guess.  First thing that I noticed was that combat felt like single button mashing most of the time. Yes, there are combos which rely on pushing the stick up, down or to the side, but you still mash X. There are special attacks, too, but the rate at which you accumulate the energy for them makes them relatively rare (and that's on normal difficulty), so not sure if I would count them. Block felt a bit inconsistent and slide as means of dodging just does not mesh with me: jumping around somehow makes a bit more sense to me. But to be fair, maybe the combat improves as you progress further.  Next thing was inconsistency with jumping. I was able to replicate it even. Sometimes I could jump multiple times in a row without stopping, sometimes - not. I noticed this when I pressed jump button on a platform that goes down almost immediately after you step on it. Obviously I fell from it. Multiple times. But the problem was that when running on the ground I was able to do two jumps just fine. But not all the time... Can be that there is a "hidden trick", and that you need to press the 2nd jump just as you are landing, I was not able to confirm that.  What bummed me the most was the story. Characters felt flat, uninspiring, and dialogues as if written by a school kid. We we also introduced a bunch of characters at once, which made them into a blur despite them being visually distinct. But style (no matter how good) is not enough to get a compelling character. Especially if they do not seem to be of consequence, and when the game is supposed to be a solo story in the first place. Not because I think so, but because it's clear from the design, since there is no feature of switching between characters (which could be cool) or calling for their aid as sort of special attacks (maybe less cool, but still could be good). And I'm not even going to complain, that this has nothing to do with PoP franchise besides some stylistic choices.  Either way, I am not getting enough motivation to finish this one. It just does not feel good to me, although some traps, that I got in the demo and in the full game were interesting, music was good, and there is good style here. But that's not enough. I was reminded of Bloodstained game, which is in similar genre, and while its story was not perfect, it was delivered in a better way, and combat (and its customization) felt more satisfying enough for me to complete the game. The Lost Crown is not like that.

I actually really enjoyed playing this game to the fact that right before final boss fight I have lost my save file due to OS reinstallation and had to play game from scratch to finish it and found myself still enjoying the game to the fullest.  This game have few mechanically very challenging parts and I was very surprised to see those in 2024 game as current days everything is easy and soft, huge plus!  I really enjoyed battle combo mechanics, very rarely you can find a platformer with combat combining almost all of the learner mechanics, very pleasant part of the game.  Overall build/progression wise this game is very close to be perfect, I wish we could have some kind of rollback mechanism to try other builds.  Map purchase mechanic is super stupid... in most cases you will find it when you have all area opened urself...  Art wise: - Music is good - Video scenes are mixed bag, some are good some are very boring and flat - Some characters drawn very good, I really enjoyed last boss. - Sounds are pretty good in some cases I had feelings were stolen from Ori, there is one sounds that is louder and very annoying than other is the gate to the weaponry upgrade godes, you run a lot near it and it's extremely annoying!  It was very annoying to loose my save file, idk why we don't have simple steam cloud sync enabled for this game.

+ Solid amount of content  + Movement is fun - Game is still full of bugs - No cloud saves - Ubisoft Connect required - Weak story and characters - Bland, forgettable boss fights  It's a serviceable metroidvania if you're looking for something to scratch that itch, but there is nothing original here. This game has a bit of an identity crisis at times and feels like it was Ubisoft's attempt at creating a game containing a collection of "greatest hits" from other successful games--all the right ingredients but lacking the nuanced touch and design choices to have them coalesce in a memorable way. I lost count of how many times I encountered something that was just a bar-for-bar stolen copy of a design or idea from a better game like Hollow Knight, Dark Souls, Celeste, or Elden Ring.

A very solid metroidvania that doesn't do anything particularly innovative but executes the metroidvania formula near perfectly.    All platforming is responsive and the game understands learning curves for new skills really well. Performing most trick sequences are incredibly satisfying. Some of the more difficult challenges took me a few times with one optional one taking me 30 minutes for less than a minute of platforming.   The combat is also nothing new. While you don’t need to technically parry the game rewards you for it. More importantly you can set your difficulty level to match what type of challenge you want with multiple things, such as parry window, to modify.   The game itself is also gorgeous and makes great use of its art style. In several areas I would stop and look at the scenery such as the gardens.   For any metroidvania fans this is one of the top games in the genre overall.

育碧虽然没落了，但多年积累的资产和经验在类银这个以独立游戏为主的赛道还是权威。 可能是目前最“现代”的类银，拍照记录对于类银游戏体验的提升是巨大的，可以回顾每个卡关点是由什么导致的卡关（能力锁、地图锁、守关怪物），针对性的寻求解法，大大节省了重复跑图的时间，也因此提升游戏的流畅度。将跑图能力和实战高度结合，这也是类银游戏里比较少见的点，几乎每一项跑图能力都有在实战中得到高效发挥的场景。游戏本身难度较高，但是内部有辅助模式，可以手动调节受伤比例，还可以pass不想玩的跳跳乐。  抛开这些“现代"的点，本身也是挑不出什么毛病的游戏，媲美动作游戏的连段（实战用处不大）和打击手感和音效，优秀的boss和锁钥设计，水准上的地图设计，美术音乐更是大厂最不可能拉胯的点（虽然给我印象不是很深），剧情和角色塑造也算无功无过。综合来看是以“现代”和战斗出彩的类银，个人可以给到90分。

这绝对是一款非常优秀的类银河恶魔城游戏，同时又兼具了波斯王子的特色，非常满意。 动作连贯，战斗爽快，弹反的反馈强烈收益高。场景也依旧贯彻了育碧优秀的美术，地图设计中上水平，而且记忆碎片功能对于探索很友好，能够显示记忆锁不能拿到的奖励的场景，不会因记不得而反复跑图。同时场景解谜难度也适中，很巧妙地将提示融于场景中，对于擅长观察环境和背景基本都很快就解出来了，唯一难的就是沙坑图里，背景上的摔跤手敌人喂给机关的提示非常隐晦。 至于跑图，很多人吐槽的跳跳乐，分为主线和挑战部分。主线跳跳乐难度算中规中矩，得益于主角很高的空中机动性，且大部分跳跳乐都能看到历代波斯王子的设计（尤其是波斯王子遗忘之沙里时间暂停和虚实交替，且是3D难度更高），相当于流畅度很高的跑酷。 挑战部分顾名思义，不在主线里，有打怪有跳跳乐，其中跳跳乐部分的难度很高，横版对比约为蔚蓝的7B往后的难度，最后的无伤跳跳乐差不多是9C难度，包括长度和背板。且大部分难度在于初见杀，不像蔚蓝可以先看路怎么走，但对主角性能熟悉后再来挑战其实也不难，而且操作感和成就感非常高。 值得一提的是藏经殿里一类的机关解谜，确实贯彻了波斯王子一贯的时间类解谜，巧妙也有意思。 （类银河恶魔城游戏基本是横版，横版游戏它就得有爽快有挑战性的跳跳乐，听说由于本作销量不佳制作组解散了实在是可惜）

Кратко: любите метроидвании - обязательно к ознакомлению! Игра отличная.  Был очень приятно удивлен этой игрой. Прошел несметное количество метроидваний и могу сказать что POP: tLC - одна из лучших метроидваний, в которые я играл. Сначала относился настороженно под влиянием общего качества продукции Юбисофт и отзывов некоторых обзорщиков (кстати, теперь категорически несогласен со многими придирками к игре), но все же купил и получил колоссальное удовольствие. Сейчас очень модно с важным видом разбирать игры на отдельные составляющие, но мало кто говорит о самом главном критерии, о критерии фана. Так вот, вся игра - сплошной фан без просадок от начала и до конца. Я прошел Silksong на 100% и, честно сказать, 3ий акт показался ненужным, утомительным и доигрывал уже с чувством утомления. POP: tLC таких ощущений не вызывает. Прошел, кстати, на предпоследнем уровне сложности не используя никакой встроенной помощи в паркуре. Сейчас думаю, что надо было проходить на последнем и очень не советую уровни сложности ниже предпоследнего, так как появится возможность танковать и не будет смысла прочувствовать все механики. Обязательно проходить туториал по боевой системе, а она прекрасна - большие возможности для комбо и красивой игры.  Визуал - великолепный. Местами дух захватывает от красоты картинки. Боевка - преотличнейшая, динамичная и позволяющая за счет навыков делать интересные связки. Враги - большая вариативность, сильная сторона игры. Паркур и паркурные загадки - тоже одна из сильных сторон игры. В меру сложно и очень весело. (Внимание: кроме "Божественных испытаний" - это тоже весело, но очень сложно и, к счастью, полностью необязательно для прохождения). Исследования - мир огромен, разнообразен и очень интересен. Музыка - очень хорошая, но больше служит подходящим фоном к событиям игры. История - слышал много придирок от обзорщиков, но мне понравилась и интрига сохранялась до конца игры. Боссы - возможно, одно из слабых мест игры. Их мало, запоминающихся всего несколько. Есть отдельная претензия к одному боссу, во время боя с которым постоянно меняется ширина обзора камеры, и это очень сильно мешает (но терпимо, в целом). Дополнение про маску - не понравилось. Во-первых, не люблю тропы с перемещением в воображаемые страны/сны/фантазии и подобное, а во-вторых дополнение сильно переусложнено в ущерб фану (душновато), в-третьих, локации показались некрасивыми. В общем, можно брать, если очень понравилась основная игра и хочется больше этой вселенной.   В общем, очень советую игру и очень жаль, что Юбисофт отменили продолжение.

This game is a pretty fun metroidvania. I engaged with the story a little deeper than most by reading all the lore pickups that you can find. The story is fairly simple, nothing to ride home about, but the cutscenes are fun to watch after adventuring for a while. Everything is all very stylish, with a few bosses exuding massive amounts of aura. The parry system is also very fun, It's instant when you push the button so you can pull off some cool rapid parries when the situation calls for it.  The platforming is really fun & the game gives you some interesting ways to traverse the environments. There are a few challenging platforming sections but nothing ever feels impossible. There's also a fair amount of puzzles that are decently challenging. You'll encounter most of these if you plan on 100% the game. For the achievement hunters, no achievement in this game is out of reach. The achievements consist of slaying bosses, finding collectables, & completing a few combat challenges. Nothing too challenging. My final verdict for Prince of Persia The Lost Crown is that you should consider picking this game up for your backlog, even more so if you can snag the game during a discount sale. You'll get a good amount of quality hours adventuring & fighting all types of monsters & ghouls on your journey throughout Mount Qaf. May the Simurgh bless your journey.

I'm deep into the second half of the game now (just beat Menolias) and wanted to drop some thoughts. Overall, it's a fantastic experience with just a few gripes holding it back from perfection.  The Good:  Story: Absolutely loving it so far.   Platforming (S-Tier): The movement is incredibly satisfying. The platforming sections are interesting, fluid, and offer a really solid challenge.  The Mixed/Needs Improvement:  Reward Economy: While the platforming is S-tier, the rewards for the hardest sections are not. Spending ages nailing a super complicated, thumb-cramping gauntlet only to be rewarded with a single Xerxes coin or some basic crystals feels pretty underwhelming. The effort-to-reward ratio is a bit off.  Combat (B-): It can feel a little clunky at times, especially when trying to nail the extremely tight parry timing. To be completely fair, I am playing on an older computer, so slight lag or frame drops might be making the parrying feel worse than it actually is.  Boss Fights: Honestly, they have been a bit too easy so far. I have cleared all the bosses up to the 50% mark in about 1 to 3 tries. I'm hoping the difficulty ramps up significantly in the second half.  Verdict: Definitely pick it up, especially if you are a fan of challenging platforming!

[i][url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/35145066/] My review page.[/url][/i]  [i][url=https://www.twitch.tv/nightserg] Follow me on twitch![/url][/i]  [h1]The gist of it:[/h1] The Prince of Persia franchise is most likely abandoned by Ubisoft, so The Lost Crown might be the last PoP game we will probably ever see, at least for the next decade. That being said, Prince of Persia, as a metroidvania, works really well, and the team behind The Lost Crown, while clearly being inspired by other similar games in the genre, good or bad, did a good job with it.   👍 [u]The good parts:[/u] +Pretty good level design +The best combat mechanics in its genre +Platforming sections were fun and fair  👎[u]The bad parts:[/u] -Difficulty slider(I'll detail below) -Hit detection of certain bosses  [h1]Story[/h1] Honestly, I came in with low expectations and got out with a decent experience when it came to its story. It's a cool take on the Prince of Persia lore, you play as one of the Immortals, and you get trapped in a time-disturbed temple of a God and you're tasked with saving someone and leaving that dreadful place. It has your typical villain that has a troubled past, your typical bro's that wanna fight you honorably, time powers, etc.   It's not super complicated, and the time travel aspect is poorly done, but other than that, it's a pretty fair run-of-the-mill story. You won't get disappointed with it, nor will you get your mind blown, I mean, it's a Prince of Persia game, none of the old ones had amazing stories either.   Story rating: 7.5/10  [h1]Gameplay[/h1] The gameplay has a lot of goods and a lot of bads, which may completely frustrate someone, or some might fully love it. I was somewhere in the middle, but more towards the better part. It's a metroidvania, just like all the ones you might've played, such as Hollow Knight. It's not as difficult, but it can be more annoying depending on the difficulty slider.   So, the difficulty slider is a super mixed bag. I get that it's accessible, but most people who play Metroidvanias don't play them to casually finish the game in a few hours and move on. The genre needs to have a well-balanced, difficult progression. Instead of that The Lost Crowns, goes for either super easy or absolute tedious, there is no middle ground regardless what choice you make. The HP on most enemies is ridiculously tedious for more than half the game if you decide to play on the hardest difficulty, among many other issues with its balancing. I prefer a game with a set difficulty by the devs, because then the game works better instead of being broken or tedious. Almost nobody in the history of gaming has ever managed to make games balanced when it comes to difficulty sliders.   Anyway, the combat mechanics are pretty good, probably the best in the genre, as you have access to a lot of melee combos, more than just hitting with a needle. Essentially, it's like the og games, but in 2D. A lot of the enemies are annoying to deal with, but you can almost cheese all of them with certain attacks. The exploration, which is the bread-and-butter of any Metroidvania, was surprisingly good, so good that I did everything 100%, which is rare for me. You'll find amulets that can completely change the outcome of the game or amulets that are completely useless, but very rarely something in the middle. Same applies to some of your super skills. The platforming in the main game and story-dlc were really amazingly done, and it felt like puzzles more than anything else.    The bosses are a mixed bag, I don't remember any particular good one, but that's because I played more than 70% of the game on Immortal, and they all felt tedious, not challenging, but massive HP sponges that took forever to kill while they could 2-3 shot you besides the fact that a lot of them have broken attack patterns that can hit you despite not actually being anywhere near them. At a certain point, I had to drop it one notch below the highest.   Avoid the challenge dlc's, the portal ones in the main base if you value your sanity. They are absolutely unfair and tedious. The platforming ones are cool, but just like the bosses, some of the spikes can hit you and reset you without even touching them.   Gameplay rating: 8/10  [h1]Audio[/h1] The voice acting was well done, nothing super remarkable, but overall well-acted. Same applies to its soundtrack and all its other sound effects, I never truly felt anything super memorable or whatnot. They fit well with the game, but not much oompfh to them.   Audio rating: 7.5/10  [h1]Graphics, performance and tech analysis[/h1] Aside from some minor bugs, the game runs phenomenally without any hiccups. Visually, it looks good, with some nice backgrounds, nice animations considering the genre, and overall a high-quality product. The best part about it is of course the level design, which many Metroidvanias don't really nail it well, but I'm glad to report that if you're a lover of exploring and finding secrets, The Lost Crown has plenty of it. The map is big, but not super huge, took me about 40 hours to 100% complete it, including the DLC.   Graphics, performance, and tech analysis score: 8.5/10  [h1]Conclusion[/h1] Honestly, it's a lot of times on sale and if you're a fan of the genre, I highly recommend it.   [h1]Final score: 7.8/10[/h1]

Story and Characters:  This is definitely the weakest part of this game.  Almost none of the characters feel complex or multi-dimensional. The character archetypes are very stereotypical and the dialog is mostly predictable. It almost feels like the game wants to target a really young audience. Motivations for why characters do what they do are often really unclear.  The story seems to try to be mythical and mysterious but just kinda ends up being a mess. Due to unclear motivations it gets more and more unclear towards the end of the game what is happening and why.  But these issues due not really harm the rest of the game too much, which is good because everything else is pretty much excellent.  Presentation:  The atmosphere, artstyle, music and sound are all quite nice. Some enemies look a bit weird, but the level visuals range from good to really stunning (Raging Sea for example).  Gameplay:  The combat system is very deep and well implemented. It feels snappy, reactive and the hitboxes feel good. It is possible to precisely duck under or jump over enemy attacks, the dash feels similar to a Dark Souls role and mastering it feels good.  Boss fights are fun to learn and the game in general seems to be pretty well balanced. On "Hero" difficulty the game was also definitely challenging enough for me, and i did not encounter any crazy difficulty spikes.  There is a lot of platforming, and its always kept fresh by new gameplay elements being added to the mix. Its really cool how many platforming mechanics the game contains. I also liked the speed at which new mechanics were introduced. Things never got boring. There are also some really difficult optional challenges.  However, controls were a mixed bag. Fighting was fine, but there were other problems:  At least on my XBox controller I had a lot of mis-inputs where my character would jump towards the wrong direction, which was at times extremely frustrating in the harder platforming sections. Also dead-zones were not properly implemented in the map view (bug?), causing the map to constantly move around when opened (but weirdly this happened only in the map).

Despues de 20h y solo con algunos coleccionables restantes por adquirir, puedo decir que es un gran metroidvania. La progresión está muy bien hecha, las habilidades están bien pensadas y son satisfactorias de utilizar, el movimiento es fluido e invita a moverse rápido, el diseño de niveles es excelente, la historia es muy interesante y con capas de profundidad, y estéticamente es precioso. Lo único que puedo decir es que tiene algunos bugs (muy pocos, no arruinan la experiencia) y que si Ubisoft decide no darte un logro, tampoco lo tendrás en Steam, cosa que me frustra un poco (me ha pasado con 2/3 logros por no tener Ubisoft Connect encendido). Me parece un juego brillante y que cumple a la perfección todo lo que se propone sin resultar repetitivo ni insipido. Tiene alma y se nota. 9/10

Me parece una verdadera lastima que este juego haya quedado tan maltratado por la reputación de ubisoft, pues en definitiva es uno de los mejores metroidvanias de la historia; toma muchas mecanicas del genero y, las dejo relucientes; tiene acertijos, plataformas y combates simplemente perfectos; una banda sonora increible, con temas que aluden a persia, pero dandoles un toque moderno con guitarras electricas e instrumentales similares.   No me cansare de recomendar el arduo trabajo que la gente detras de ubisoft montpellier hizo con este juegazo.

This is a very good game, and one of the best metroidvanias out there. I liked it similarly to Ori and Nine Sols. Based on the reviews I thought it would not be very good, but I liked a lot (I think some of the bad reviews were not about the game itself). The graphics are very good, there is a lot of content, and I liked the skills that are unlocked.   The platforming is challenging but very good. The enemies are fine, and the bosses are hard but not so much (different than Hollow Knight). You can change the difficulty to be easier, although I did that only for the last boss (and last special boss) of the Mask of Darkness DLC, that is harder than the base game, I considered the default difficulty on point for all the base game, not too easy, and not too hard.

This game is so close to being very good.. so close. Unfortunately, the design leans way too hard into punishing platforming, which I get is supposed to be the staple for PoP, but, in this case, it's frustrating and not fun. The number of excruciatingly long platforming segments where one small mistake will lead to having to start all over is just way over the top. The game constantly expects you to react to unforeseen obstacles at the last second, flawlessly, or you get to start the entire section over again. You'll be doing this over and over again. Who finds that fun? Not me, and I love these kinds of games.  The combat isn't quite there, either. It, again, is just not fun. There's something that just feels unrefined about it. Enemies don't telegraph their attacks nearly well enough and half of the time you'll be taking cheap shots that simply don't feel like you were given a fair chance at avoiding to begin with.   This game has potential but they simply went too far with it. I think they know this because of the exceptionally high amount of difficulty modifiers they've put in. Unfortunately, these don't really solve the core issues with the design and I can't bring myself to play it any longer than I have.

Великолепная игра.   Сразу вспомнилась старая Ubisoft, которая могла в геймплей и самобытность. Сначала немного напрягал странный дизайн персонажей, но, через пять минут я забыл, что играю в принца Персии, расслабился и начал получать удовольствие.   Собственно геймплей представляет из себя стандартную метроидванию, с отличным управлением, жесткими таймингами и действительно интересными загадками. Боев не то чтобы много, элемент исследования выражен здесь гораздо сильнее. Особенно понравилось обилие жесткого платформинга в DLC и на некоторых локациях. Из минусов - разве что наличие учётки Uplay для игры в стим. Один раз сервис у Юбиков прилег отдохнуть и мои сейвы не смогли синхронизироваться с облаком.

A damned fine metroidvania that's a joy artistically to look and listen to, keeps you on your toes with a great suite of combat and traversal abilities and brings the joy of exploring a bigger-than-expected interconnected map.  Special shoutout to the suite of quality of life & accessiblity options - the ability to make a screenshot that's then added as a pin to the map is an innovation I'd be happy to see spread over other exploration-focused games. Being able to remind youself visually why you couldn't explore an area was a great boon.  It's biggest weakness was the story that, while it has intriguing time curse shenanigans at its heart and  does make good use of a repeat villain, has just too many plot lines start and fizzle out unsatisfyingly to land at anything better than an "okay".

[h3] Fantastic revolutionary gameplay that isn't matched by its poor story [/h3]   I've played a lot of 2.5D games out there on my not so high end laptop, but this is the first Metroidvania game I've ever played, I liked the idea of open world 2D world map with fast time travels, what draw my attention to this game is the gameplay, the gameplay is revolutionary probably one of the best in the 2.5D gaming market if not the best out there, custom difficulty, the ability to change the difficulty at the save game runtime itself without starting a new game, treasure map (purchasable later or after you beat the game they are totally optional the game gives you a variety of ways to look for hidden items early on) , 2D maps, this is the first time I've experienced something like that and man, in some way the gameplay mechanics of this game saved me hours of watching YouTube videos on how to unlock secret items and treasures, there's also levelling system, upgrade system, treasure loot, shop system, grinding system and some slight light RPG mechanics through the amulet system where you can customize your character and equip it with different abilities.   I still have some criticism, the game lacks cheat codes, also while the customizable difficulty fixes some broken or so hard boss fights, there are a lot of difficult parkour hard to reach area with no cheat codes to workaround it, one of the most annoying one was the impossible climb yeah that is broken as hell, and you must collect all bells at once not one at a time, that is just stupid, you should provide a cheat code method to workaround hard to reach areas something like unlimited jumps for example would be helpful.   Other 2.5D game developers must take some important notes from this game, also you should provide a way to play old cutscenes, and you should provide the ability to battle side mini smaller bosses the same way we can fight main bosses.   [h3]Excellent Compat system[/h3]  Combat in this game is top notch, there's dodging, parrying and something called vengeful parrying, where you parry a special enemy talk and you follow it with a deadly counter attack of your own, works even on some bosses.   [h3]poor unconnected story [/h3]   The storyline is where the game falls too hard, the first half is phenomenal, it concentrates about a conflict for the throne of Persia with enemies outside and inside Persia seeking to control it, you got the Kushans an outside invaders and you got inside enemies who are plotting a coup to overthrew the current king and take the throne for themselves, the game is filled with politics, betrayal, backstabbing, twists and turns at least the first half, the second half is pure trash it diverges away from the first half of the story, where basically the main antagonist of the game wants to acquire a super power to destroy the world and start a new, this is a lazy meaningless poorly trash writing, not to mention the ridiculous strange power scaling to out for the trash plot, and for some reason the main antagonist decides to [spoiler] leave Persia and its political conflict behind [/spoiler] I don't know the story can't match the revolutionary gameplay.   [h3]poor marketing [/h3]   I heard about this game from ChatGPT, I asked ChatGPT to list some demanding heavy hardware resources 2.5D games and it listed prince of Persia, when I looked the game I was blown away by its amazing graphics and levelling system,   unfortunately I won't buy The rouge Prince of Persia, due to its low budget, low graphics quality and lack of RPG elements and overall very simple design, also cancelling sequels was a wrong decision a better decision maybe is to delay sequels till the game reaches the expected desired revenue, also the game sold 3 millions copies, I don't know what was Ubisoft expecting with this 2.5D game.  I also won't buy 2.5D Assassin's creed games because stealth doesn't work for 2.5D games,   Prince of Persia The Lost Crown was the near perfect 2.5D game that attracted my attention.   It's really sad that Ubisoft disbanded the team behind this masterpiece of a game, Ubisoft must pay some serious attention to 2.5D gaming as there are a lot of people who like simplicity also there are a lot of people who don't own a high end graphics card capable of running AAA titles so they will turn into lighter 2.5D games instead,   9/10 for gameplay.  4/10 for storyline.

Як же в мене горіла ср@ка в певні періоди проходження! В той час я лаяв гру за те що вона як пародія на "Ori and..." та на дарк соулси всілякі, та за просто неймовірну кількість т. зв. бектрекінгу; а тому хто зробив кімнату де ти протягом трьох хвилин і сотні спроб мусиш ухилятися від пилок щоб отримати у винагороду нові штани для персонажа має бути окрема жаровня у пеклі. Сюди ж відносяться і інші лабиринти шипів і палаючого гузна.  Окремий пункт - повістка. Ну припустимо, чорношкірий парубок на службі корони - ок для Персії. (Дякую за скін Принца Персії 2008). Жінка-коваль це абсурдно. Жінка-наставниця ГГ в той час як серед Безсмертних є більш вправні Ваграм і Артабан? Вона Саргона мабуть гачком в'язати вчила? Я поважаю жінок але всі повинні грати Свої соціальні ролі, навіть в іграх.  Вцілому я раджу грати гру якщо ви не чутливі до високих температур нижче спини або не будете, як я, намагатись проходити її на 100%. Хороший сожет про зраду і прощення, цілком не погана музика і візуал. (Локація з розбитими коряблями застиглими у часі - моя окрема повага художникам) Гра хороша, хоча, я був би більш радий добротному ремейку Prince Of Persia 2008.  PS: Гра добре шуршить на Лінукс і до неї є ідеальний на 98% Українізатор. Шкода що нативно немає Української.

[h1]Шикарный платформер-метроидвания с кучей механик и черным Принцем Персии.[/h1]  Сражаемся с чудищами и богами в 2D цветастом экшене от разрабов Rayman Legends и Rayman Origins. Вышло мега годно, проходил на релизе в стиме на одном дыхании, и сейчас быстренько пробежал сюжетное DLC, которое там выходило. Игра напомнила мне самые первые части Принца Персии, но в современной графике, с кучей интересных для платформера механик и боссов.  Сюжет сейчас помню слабо, но он присутствует, и не чисто для галочки. Семеро бессмертных воинов защищают Персию, один из которых наш Принц. Есть небольшие кат-сценки, русские субтитры. Помимо основных квестов есть также побочные. Локации большие, с разным ландшафтом.  Геймплейно это прям сок для любителей платфомеров и метроидваний. Куча механик для паркура: двойные прыжки, подкаты, бег по стенам, притягивание крюком-кошкой и многое другое. Можно оставлять свою временную копию и потом на нее телепортироваться. Но при этом и много приемов для боя: разные комбинации приемов, блоки, парирования, ультимейты, которые можно получить из боссов, стрельба из лука. Чередуются сражения и по большей части платформинг, иногда встречаются небольшие головоломки. Восстанавливать здоровье можно у деревьев.  Игра выполнена в приятных цветастых тонах, имеет красочные анимации, которые при этом не отвлекают от происходяшего на экране, что нередко бывает в данном жанре игр. Боссов в игре не так уж много, как хотелось бы, но выполнены они качественно. Можно переигрывать сражения прямо из главного меню на скорость для каждого уровня сложности. Сложность тут гибкая, но ее, вроде бы, нельзя сменить по ходу игры. Впрочем, она влияет только на сражения, которые тут не такие сложные. Наибольшую сложность может вызвать именно составляющая платформера, особенно в DLC. Впрочем, для любителей жанра, особых сложностей не будет.  Отдельно по DLC Mask of Darkness. Я взял Complete Upgrade пак, куда входит DLC, но стим считает, что его у меня нет. Поэтому отдельно туда обзор написать, как планировал, не могу, и оставлю вкратце здесь. DLC - чистый платформинг, причем всю прокачку и умения забирают. Это побочное задание, которое можно получить где-то в середине игры. Так как у меня слетели сейвы, пришлось ставить готовое, чтобы сразу отправиться в дополнение. Там всего два босса и немного сражений. В основном это хардкорный побег от глаза, который летает за вами. Сохранений немного, однако есть контрольные точки в случае падений, где снимают совсем немного хп. Проходится за пару часов. Мне понравилось, но оно значительно сложнее основной игры, хотя там есть кое-какие испытания и посложнее. Брать на свой страх и риск, если у вас хороший опыт игры в платформеры. При частых падениях там предлагают какой-то режим упрощенных прыжков для помощи, но я его так и не попробовал.  [h2]Итог[/h2] Это, вероятно, лучшая метроидвания и один из лучших 2D платформеров, в которые я играл. Несмотря на повестку в лице черного Принца, исследовать мир игры и проходить - одно удовольствие. В меру сложная, в меру продолжительная. Красивые анимации, много различных локаций для исследования. Сюжет эпичный и интересный, с путешествиями во времени и сражении с богами и мифическими чудовищами. Для любителей платформеров рекомендую обратить внимание на игру, даже если вы не знакомы с Принцем Персии. Играть лучше на геймпаде - управление на нем очень удобное. DLC - это чистый платформинг, более хардкорный, чем сама игра.   [h2]✅ Понравилось:[/h2] - Отличный платформер с кучей механик и хорошим управлением - Цветастая приятная стилистика с анимациями, которые не отвлекают от происходящего - Разнообразные приемы для сражений, настраиваемая сложность - Много боссов, можно переигрывать из меню на скорость на разных сложностях  [h2]❌ Не понравилось:[/h2] - Были небольшие просадки в оптимизации, вероятно из-за Denuvo

I have always had a soft spot for the Prince of Persia games, going all the way back to the original in the 80's. This is just a perfect little game. I'm playing it on SteamDeck, so it's fantastic to just pick up, play it for a bit and put it down. And still feel like you have made progress, thanks to the great save system.  I thought I would get overwhelmed by the open world, but the ability to screen shot areas, with markers, or items you can't reach yet, fix that issue for me. I think if there is any issue it's Ubisoft, making me having to be online to link in with their portal. As I said, I play this on SteamDeck, and I don't always have access to WiFi, and I don't want to use my mobile phone as a hotspot, just to play this game. Talking of Ubisoft it's a shame that this fab game, and the studio (now defunct) who made it were a victim of poor marketing by Ubisoft. The demo which I played prior to buying, also did not make me want to buy the game. Because the demo to me made it just look like a massive open world, challenging platformer. But as said before, the actual game makes that a non issue.

Assuming you don't have problems with Ubisoft Connect being installed and getting logged in.... (yes, you have to bite that particular bullet here unfortunately).  This game gets a bit of a bad rep because... well... its artistic design is, shall we say, a bit off key.  It doesn't even star a protagonist who is a Prince of Persia.  It's kind of problematic like that.  That said, the dlc does include a couple character skins that are much more fitting for a "Prince of Persia" and fit the theme better.  It's too bad about the way this was received in general, because honestly this is a really great game.  The action is snappy and fun, the platforming is fun and interesting, the world is beautiful, the music is good, the voice acting is good, the story is decent... this is true AAA quality.  I'm really enjoying the game so far, and I feel like its a Metroidvania made for the masses rather than for a select few hardcore punishment fanatics like "ahem" some other famous Metroidvanias.  This one has really great options for customizing the challenge and overall I've found it super rewarding.  The combat here can really push you if you crank the difficulty.  This game also has some very nice QoL options like map markers as well as screenshot markers, which really help with backtracking.  Helps prevent you wasting time.  It also has two modes, one where it highlights your next objective for the main quest, and one where it doesn't.  You still get hints in your log, but it just kind of lets you explore and figure it out.  I love that.  Options for everyone, so no matter which way you enjoy it you have what you like.  Very well done in that regard.  Two of my favorite games are Ori 1 and 2 and, while I wouldn't quite put this on par with those, it's actually closer than most games on the market and that's saying a lot.  I do recommend trying it out if you haven't... it's quite a great feel for the genre.

Prince of Persia als 2D Plattformer ist eine ziemlich coole Idee und die Umsetzung ist wirklich gelungen. Das Bewegen fühlt sich gut an, mir kommt es aber so vor, als ob vereinzelt Eingaben nicht richtig erkannt werden (Richtung vom Dash, Absprünge).  Der Kartenaufba ist zu Beginn etwas verwirrend aber ansonsten normal und passend für ein Metroidvania.   Kämpfen fühlt sich zu Beginn ebenfalls etwas clunky an, aber nachdem ich das Training [spoiler] in der Zuflucht [/spoiler] gemacht hatte war ich abgeholt. Es gibt echt coole Kombos und Optionen seine Feinde zu erledigen. Gruppen von Gegner sind etwas anstrengend, da man sie oft schlecht parieren kann, es gibt aber theoretisch genug Möglichkeiten. Wenn man wie ich kurz vorher Nine Sols gespielt hat ist man es nur anders gewöhnt.   Story ist bisher simpel aber gut. Kann sich noch in verschiedene Richtungen entwickeln und daher (für mich) nicht vorhersehbar.   Negativ anzumerken ist die Notwendigkeit eines Ubisoft-Kontos, weswegen ich mir erst Jahre später geholt habe, obwohl ich sogar ein Konto zum Zeitpunkt der Veröffentlichung besaß.   Meiner Meinung nach trotzdem sehr empfehlenswert!  Edit: Was mir auch immer wieder positiv aufgefallen ist, ist die deutsche Sprachausgabe. Hierbei können zwar nicht alle Charaktere glänzen, aber vor allem Sargon ist sehr gut gesprochen.

Ce MetroidVania estampillé Prince of Persia est plutôt convaincant pour les amateurs du genre tel que moi. Le cocktail action/plateforme fonctionne à merveille sans que l'un ne prenne trop le pas sur l'autre. J'ai trouvé le jeu un poil trop facile mais avec 4 niveaux de difficulté disponible, je pense que chacun peut y trouver son compte.  Les mouvements du prince sont très fluide et il est agréable à contrôler. Sa montée en puissance est très grisante en fin de partie malgré qu'elle soit très peu convaincante au début. J'ai vraiment eu l'impression qu'on me donnait juste des pouvoirs "prétextes" à des puzzles ni très variés ni très intéressants alors qu'un simple et classique double saut (que l'on débloque par la suite) rend le jeu plus nerveux et procure un sentiment de puissance bien plus tangible.  L'autre chose m'ayant pas mal déroutée a été la "construction" du monde. Tout au long de ma partie, j'ai eu la sensation d'un amas d'idées (souvent bonnes) mais qui ne s'enrichissent pas au fil du jeu. Chaque zone à son gimmick et reste en vase clos. Dommage. Et si on ajoute à ça un manque de transition marquante entre chacune des zones (même si certaines le sont : Port-Noyé/Mer Déchaînée), ça renforce ce côté vase clos désagréable. Enfin, les combats sont très satisfaisants. Esquives, parades, coups spéciaux, combos, tout y passe et les petites cinématiques en combat (bien qu'un peu répétitives parfois) apportent vraiment un souffle épique à l'ensemble. Les boss ne sont ni très nombreux, ni très compliqués et c'est peut-être un point que les plus hardcore d'entre nous jugeront négativement mais ça m'a largement suffit. Petit bémol cependant, malgré la tonnes de breloques de collier disponibles je n'en ai à peine utiliser qu'une fraction et idem pour les coups spéciaux (3 utilisés sur 10 au total). Et je ne suis pas le seul puisque seul 8% des joueurs steams avaient débloqué le succès consistant à utiliser tous les coups spéciaux. Ça a sans doute à voir avec la difficulté mais je laisserais les plus puristes d'entre nous critiquer plus précisément ce point là.

育碧这次确实打了一场漂亮的翻身仗。这款游戏不仅是“波斯王子”系列的回归，更是类银河恶魔城游戏的诚意之作：  量大管饱：内容极其充实，探索解谜与战斗的比例拿捏得恰到好处，几十小时的流程毫无尿点。  丝滑体验：画面美术风格独特，且优化顶级。无论战斗还是平台跳跃，高帧率带来的流畅感非常解压。  人性化之王：最值得称赞的是其近乎奢侈的难度自定义。它没有高高在上的“受苦”架子，通过精细的调节选项（如伤害数值、跳台辅助），让反应速度稍慢或追求休闲的中老年玩家也能通过“逃课”或“降低门槛”享受纯粹的成就感。  这是一款真正尊重玩家时间的硬核作品，它证明了硬核游戏也可以通过设计变得温情而包容。

[h2] Вкусно, но пусто. ★★★★★★★☆☆☆ 7,5/10 🌟[/h2]  [b] Наверное, лучшая игра от юбиков, которая выходила за последние несколько лет. [/b] Отличный визуал, динамичная боевка и приятный платформинг.  [b] Сюжет — шляпа, он просто существует и совсем не цепляет. [/b] Если бы его подтянули до уровня геймплея, игра заиграла бы намного ярче. Хотя сами сюжетные вставки и анимации сделаны достойно.  Боссы весьма легкие, и в битвах с ними используются механики всех способностей игры, так что скучно не будет. Правда, парирование в основной игре я не так часто и использовал — легче было через перекаты играть.  [u] DLC — говнище, просто дно. [/u] Единственное достоинство — финальный босс. Он нереально жирный и заставляет использовать парирование на максимум, чтобы не получить по лицу. Брать этот доп. контент — ошибка.  [b] Это отличная игра, [/b] к которой стоит присмотреться, даже несмотря на то, что она не о самом Принце. Обидно только за команду разработчиков: их расформировали по другим проектам компании, и вряд ли она увидит продолжение.
