Ultimately I didn't like this game enough to complete it, but that doesn't mean I can't recommend it. POP The Lost Crown has fantastic movement and platforming, cool aerial combat, and a pretty good (if predictable) story. Plus full voice acting and cutscenes. My issues were more personal than objective:  The map is almost too big. The levels feel endless and monotonous - I had to keep checking my map to figure out where I was going because there are very few landmarks and every part of a given level feels the same. Compare to games like Salt and Sanctuary or Hollow Knight where it's much easier to figure out where you're heading based on environmental features.  The aerial combat is really hard and I couldn't get particularly good at it. The game does have extensive difficulty sliders and I mostly played on easy, but I probably would have had more fun if I could have gotten better and did the fights on hard mode. If you like fighting games you will love this. You even get limit break-style ultimate moves.  Recommended even at full price with the above minor caveats. The game is excellent. Sorprendido por el tremendo juegazo que es este Prince of Persia, me hizo volver al pasado al jugar por primera vez al Prince of Persia en una computadora con windows 98, con esa dificultad que caracterizaba los juegos antiguos, saltos milimétricos, correr a través de estructuras que te podían matar si no calculabas los tiempos, atravesar espacios sin saber lo que habrás más adelante y que lo combina con lo mejor que tuvo la saga Prince of persia con la trilogía de las arenas del tiempo, todo eso y sumado a un combate bien hecho, buena historia, animación fluida, visualmente hermoso y puzzles bien pensados. Con decir que me pasé los desafíos que son opcionales a la historia principal y que hasta cierto punto pueden tomar muchas horas para pasarlo por su excesiva dificultad y aún así terminé satisfecho. Lástima que Ubisoft lo tenga, y también un poco lamentable que no haya tenido la recepción que se merecía cuando salió y más que seguro por eso cancelaron el remake de las Arenas del Tiempo. Pero sin más que decir, esta es una joya que nadie se debería perder y más si son fans de la saga que por el bien del gaming debería volver en todo su esplendor. 10/10

虽然我推荐这款游戏，但是我要说： 1、跳跳乐是需要单手操作的！！！重要的事情自己脑补三遍！！！在蔚蓝玩家看起来难度属于路边一条的跳跳乐，可以卡半天，原因全部来自于单手操作，左手中间三指控制方向没办法，空格键控制跳跃没办法，尤其重要的冲刺键保留给你的小拇指，随便一个跳跳乐，左手按的头皮发麻的情况下，右手在还能叼支烟。 2、游戏观看对话是需要双手操作的。你的单手跳跳乐跳完了吗？终于轮到你右手操作了，回车键+鼠标左键看对话简直是天才，一段对话出来，你空格键拍烂了也不带动一下的。 3、冲刺键在地面上默认是向后冲刺！！！在空中默认向前冲刺！！！地面上向前冲刺（滑铲）是前+冲刺！！！ 4、看起来体型巨大的BOSS，感觉冲刺没办反闪到身后的是错觉，可以轻松闪过去；看起来随便一个拿盾的小怪，感觉可以轻松闪过去的也是错觉，因为压根就闪不过去。 5、鼠标存在的意义仅仅是射箭瞄准。噢，不对，还有那完全不明所以的键盘加鼠标联合释放技能的设定。总共就没几个技能，硬是设计出格挡键+鼠标键这种天才操作。DNF策划应该给你们点赞。 6、正常难度（勇士）的小怪，刀刀烈火。你说你魂系设定吧，存档点出门一个不小心给你干到只剩血皮，好，拿上你的5个水晶，回去存个档，出门继续干小怪。‘  总结：这款游戏可以作为右脑开发训练平台

This really feels like an all-timer to me, a Hollow Knight tier experience that sits easily near the top of my favorites in the genre. It has the sauce... slick movement mechanics with tight controls, engaging combat, solid exploration and set pieces, and the length is just right.  My only complaints would be minor QoL ones... the game could use a 'return to last tree' menu button, for example.  It's so disappointing that this is hobbled by a mess of Ubisoft connect integration, broken save syncing, lack of family share, no future patching for minor bugs (I killed a quest enemy on a slope and could never loot his drop, for example), and the knowledge that Ubisoft would rather break up the team which worked on it and refuse a proposed sequel than just simul-release the game on steam and actually market it.  It doesn't deserve that kind of treatment.

Menudo juegazo se han marcado los de Ubisoft, está claro que cuando se ponen a hacer juegos que no son triples A, les salen geniales, como el Child of Light, por ejemplo. Este se acaba de convertir en el mejor metroidvania que he jugado nunca. Lo tiene todo, puzzles muy buenos, jugabilidad endiablada, combate perfecto (de hecho he jugado los mejores combates contra jefes que recuerdo), backtracking inmejorable, y encima, en lo que suelen flaquear muchos metroidvanias, en este cumple perfectamente, que es en la historia. Así que no puedo estar mas contento de haberlo jugado. Uno de mis primeros juegos de mi vida fue el Pince of Persia original, y este es una dignísima secuela. Curiosamente lo he acabado justo cuando EEUU ha atacado Irán, y me da mucha pena donde ha terminado Irán, y lo que era hace 50 años sin el dominio de la religión, y ni te cuento ya cuando era Persia. Mi nota es un 9,5.

메트로바니아로서 재미는 있는데 좀 짜증남. 편의성이 부족하고 도전에 대한 보상이 박한 느낌. 명작이라고 해서 일단 사봤는데, 한참 전에 나온 블러드 스테인드 리추얼 오브 나이트보다 낫다는 생각이 들지를 않음.  일단 세이브 포인트가 지정된 공간, 화톳불 같은 곳에서만 가능한데 이게 상당히 멀리 떨어져 있어 죽을 때마다 답답함. 죽고나면 밝혔던 맵이나 모았던 돈은 그대로이긴하나, 마지막 부활지점에서 리스폰되며, 죽은 지점으로 돌아가기까지 다시 되돌아가야하다보니 죽음의 리스크가 크게 느껴지고 까놓고 말해서 죽음 재도전 과정이 굉장히 귀찮음. 몹들도 리젠되다보니 한번에 막히지 않고 뚫리는게 아니라면 내가 게임을 진행한다는 느낌을 못받는 부분도 존재하는데, 대다수의 모든 게임들은 도전을 통해 시행착오를 겪고 성공으로 향한 즐거운 과정을 누려야 한다고 생각함. 근데 적어도 이 게임은 그런 부분에 있어서 거리감이 좀 떨어져있음.   90년대 개발된 컴퓨터 사면 부록으로 주는 30개 가량의 게임들조차 조작키를 바꿀 수 있는데 2024년에 나온 게임이 조이스틱 위주의 플레이를 권장한다는 이유만으로 키보드 마우스 키세팅 변경이 불가능한 것은 정말 이해가 안가는 부분중 하나이고,  액션게임으로서 패링 적 띄우거나 낙하 공격 등 다양한 액션을 보여주는 반면  성장요소는 체력, 장비칸에 해당하는 악세사리, 무기나 악세사리 강화정도인데 레벨 개념이 따로 존재하지 않고 성장 요소 자체도 그러하다보니, 3시간가량을 플레이했음에도 불구하고 성장이 크게 체감이 잘 되지 않음. 나름 길도 열심히 개척했고 목걸이 장식템도 얻고 1차 보스전도 이겨냈는데 게임에서 세이브 파일 접속할 때 뜨는 탐험도 외에는 내가 뭘하긴 했나 체감이 좀 약함. 끽해야 특정 게이지 모아 사용하는 기술 추가되는 게 체감이 그나마 되긴 함.  낙사나 가시에 있어서 모 파란 로봇처럼 즉사는 아니라서 다행이기는 한데... 이것도 솔직히 좀 애매함.   특정 플랫포머 공간에서 실수로 닿일 경우, 그 구간 처음부터 다시 해야하며  hp랑 포션마저 없다? 그럼 부활지점으로 돌아가 그 지점을 다시 해야하는 상황이 벌어짐. 액션을 강조하고 플랫포머를 강조한건 좋은데 순간이동이라던가 가까운 거리에서 부활정도는 추가한다고 해서 메트로바니아 빠들이 욕할것 같지는 않은데.   또 하나 개인적으로 아쉬운 점은 스토리. 과장해서 표현하면 우연히 특이한 현상을 조우한 if 역사물 같음. 까놓고 말해서 재미가 없음. 처음 2시간. 스팀에서 환불할까 말까 고민하는 시간이며, 이 게임을 더 할지 말지 결정하는 매우 중요한 시간임에도 불구하고 전혀 알차게 보내지 못했음. 메트로바니아로서 특징을 잘 갖췄고 액션성도 있지만 후반을 위해 레벨디자인을 초반에 의도적으로 불편하거나 성장의 체감이 잘 안되게 만든게 느껴지는데, 그럼 이걸 스토리의 흥미진진함, 주인공의 매력, 앞으로 어떻게 될까 이런 요소로 답답한 구간을 수월하게 넘기도록 만들었어야 함.  그냥 주인공이 짱센 설정을 가지고 있고 모험을 하면 충분하겠지 라는 안이한 의식이 느껴질 정도. 슈퍼마리오에서 쿠파에게 잡혀간 피치공주 구하러가는게 차라리 좀더 스토리에 몰입감이 있겠네. 겐 차라리 여자이기라도 하고, 쿠파는 우리가 항상 상징적으로 두들겨야할 최종보스니까. 의문점, 미스터리함을 살짝 주기는 하는데 감정 메마른 용병마냥 초현실적인 상황을 마주하고 왕자가 납치당하는 전개가 벌어졌음에도 불구하고 스토리텔링 면에서 전혀 살려내지 못했음.
Bu oyunun Ubisoft'dan çıkıyor olması şaşırtıcı.

Huge Prince of Persia fan here, so all the PoP information in the last few months isn't looking good. Regardless this game is 100% worth it (on sale). I would never justify the retail price of $50/60. Currently it being on sale and the price cut now is a great time to enjoy it.  The game fulfills the "prince" feeling I had decades ago, and just in the start, the combat is satisfying, the exploration is great so far, and with that the "Memory" system is something that should be implemented in all Metroidvanias.  Biggest downfall of this game - ubisoft publisher  Post a lot of playtime: I am actually in awe with how well this game is made. I said before the combat is great, and my that was an understatement. Same goes with exploration - this game legitimately has some of the best platforming I have ever done, you feel like an actual Prince in control of time aspects and they absolutely nailed it!  The more I play this game, the more sad I am they scrapped the team behind this because it didn't meet "expectations"... Lower your expectations then ubisoft, or fail trying - AAA greed at it's finest

[h1] A solid metroidvania. [/h1]  Before starting the game myself I had read about it being rather good but I think I can say it certainly has exceeded my expectations.   [h3] The gameplay [/h3] The most important part and it is [b]peak[/b]. The fluidity of movement especially, it makes platforming a very pleasant experience and guarantees a good time with the game. I cannot help but compare it to Ori games - both gave me similar vibe and pure joy when traversing their respective landscapes.  [h3] The combat is...[/h3]  ...alright. It never gave me much trouble (I played on the second-to-highest difficulty because the Immortal one is a bit ridiculous and while I do like challenge, I am too old for damage-sponge type of it) and yet I wouldn't call it the greatest; it just is. The game would be definitely worse without it though.   As for the   [h3]graphics and soundtrack:[/h3] top notch. Not much to be said, the game is pretty and sounds nice; however, there were sometimes visual glitches here and there (mostly missing background between assets).   And for the worst part:  [h3] the story[/h3] It is all over the place. I am trying to not be very picky about those and yet sometimes in the Lost Crown the story felt like it was written as an assignment by a middle-school student who has just read their first adventure book. E.g. [spoiler] We run around with these feathers that give us powers for the whole game, right? And JUST BY THE END OF IT we get told that they hurt us. And it is never brought up again. Why.[/spoiler] There were several times when I questioned the logic behind some dialogues, it may be due to cut content, I don't know. It just isn't that good.   [h3]Overall[/h3] it took me about 25 hours to 100% the game (with DLC) and I had a really great time with it. If you are looking for a great metroid- or even a -vania, pick it up, it's totally worth it.

Лучшая игра Ubisoft за последнее время. В неё очень приятно играть: она не наказывает слишком жёстко в платформинге, а после гибели не отбирает всю валюту. Игра постоянно поддерживает интерес к исследованию локаций за счёт нахождения наград для улучшения персонажа и раскрытия лора.  Интересные элементы платформинга делают прохождение уровней увлекательным. Во время прохождения даются новые способности, что позволяет разнообразить исследование локаций и решать интересные головоломки.  Боевая система приятная. Хотя она упрощённо-слешерная, но позволяет комбинировать приёмы, буквально превращая врагов в мячик. Особенно весело сражаться с боссами: они уникальные, и к каждому приходится искать свою тактику.  Сюжет тоже порадовал — он интересный, главный герой со временем меняется, а мотивы главного злодея понятны. Интересно читать записки и диалоги с другими персонажами.  Это лучшая метроидвания за последнее время. За всё прохождение игра ни разу не наскучила и не раздражала, а только сильнее затягивала.

I really love the quality of life features that introduced in this game to aid both metroidvania fans and casuals find enjoyment in this game.  Things like being able to save screenshots to your map for later reference, and options to provide additional indicators and guidance to your map if you get lost are huge improvements to keep pushing people forward towards completing the game.  Metroidvania-style game developers should take note of these quality-of-life improvements and run with it.  Furthermore, while this is a 2D (as in sidescroller) game, it really pushes forward with the art direction, full voice acting, and cinematics that really propel the genre forward.  It's a fully realized game.  It's challenging but fun.  It has a ton of options, and whether you want a "hardcore" metroidvania discovery experience, a more guided casual experience, are a speedrunner, or a seasoned gamer that craves a heavy challenge - The team behind The Lost Crown has clearly put in the effort to build a welcoming tent for all gamers.  Big +1 from me, and I hope that other metroidvania style game developers take note.    It's really sad that the reports are that this game has under-sold expectations, because I want to see more like this.  Ubisoft or not, this is a quality offering.

Even though I have around 25+ hrs on this, I must give it a bad review. If a game requires a launcher and an account from Ubisoft, then just move on to the next game since the launcher and Ubisoft's own services do not work. Ubisoft's authentication service will not work on a normal Wi-fi connection and will need to be done via mobile data. The launcher is intrusive and annoying as well.   Now the amount of game breaking bugs and softlocks in this game is honestly astounding. I'm surprised these have not been addressed up until now. You can sometimes freeze mid animation, certain items will cause the game to crash if you interact with them, certain events will make you invulnerable and get to places you're not supposed to get to, and then you will have to restart your whole game since there is no turning back. Spoilers ahead::   Moving on to gameplay. The combat is smooth and satisfying but the difficulty curve is horribly inconsistent and too punishing for no reason. The platforming is honestly the highlight; very fluid movement with very well-designed and balanced challenges (mostly...).   Now the story meanders and you can catch the general gist of an attempt at coherence, but ultimately it feels too big for its own good and didn't focus on the core idea which also hurt the gameplay loop. Flipping the Prince of Persia IP and having the antagonist with the time powers made this very intriguing, but unfortunately failed to translate into a deeper gaming narrative. You collect the standard metroidvania powers (All of them for some reason) and these are meant to help you beat the prince, yet none of them seem to actually do anything in the game to counter that set of time power abilities. The writers and designers seem to have written themselves into a corner as they couldn't produce a set of abilities that would make sense from a gameplay perspective to serve as a counter to time powers. I do not think that a double jump, grapple hook and teleportation would hinder a being with deity-like control over time. That is ultimately proven correct in the climax of the story where you beat the prince with the power of friendship.   The setting and art is beautiful and there are some locations which are absolutely gorgeous with the highlight being the Raging Sea frozen in time. Art team knocked it out of the park with the exception of the main character's hair. I do not get where the hell can someone get a haircut like that in Persia but okay sure, I'll wave it off as another one of those 'Artist took too much artistic freedom'.   Prince of Persia The Lost Crown narrative and worldbuilding suffers from being bloated by too many ideas and execution mainly falters because of that. It's trying to cram anything tangentially related to Persia into the mix from Persian dynasties, mythology, Zoroastrianism. middle eastern cosmology, folktales and so on. The mix just feels flat and not very engaging, like a backdrop to justify a setting without actually immersing you in it.   The music also is very blind and not memorable.   Finally, something which actually left me more angry and thinking that there must have been a whole bunch of idiots and bigots at Ubisoft that signed off on this, is the presence of suicide bombers in the game. Is that what Ubisoft thinks of it's middle eastern audience? That this is some caricature of the devs think of that audience, or is this a ♥♥♥♥ You to the same culture that you want to appropriate, repackage and sell?   ♥♥♥♥ you Ubisoft.

I went into Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown with very low expectations and came out pretty hooked. I genuinely did not expect this level of quality or polish. This ended up being one of the best rogue like experiences I have had in a long while and easily one of my favorite action platformers overall.  The moment to moment gameplay just feels incredible. Combat is fast, smooth, and very fluent. The combo system gives you a lot of freedom and creativity. Juggling enemies, parrying attacks, chaining abilities together, and unleashing Athra skills. Fights feel stylish without being button mashy, and once everything clicks you feel unstoppable. Boss battles especially are fantastic and demand real mastery instead of cheap tricks.  Platforming is just as strong. Movement is tight and precise, which makes the tougher sections extremely satisfying to overcome. Some sequences are genuinely challenging and require full concentration. If you enjoy difficult platforming gauntlets, this game absolutely delivers.  Exploration is another highlight. Mount Qaf is large but never bloated. Every area feels handcrafted and visually distinct, packed with secrets, upgrades, cosmetics, and meaningful rewards. I never felt like I was collecting filler. The screenshot map feature is also great and something I wish every Metroidvania had.  The DLC The Mask of Darkness raises the difficulty even further. It is noticeably tougher than the base game and feels aimed at players who really want to test their skills. If you thought the main game was challenging, the DLC will push you pretty hard.  Performance wise, the game is extremely polished. Everything runs smoothly and feels responsive. It is just a very well made product from start to finish. Huge credit to Ubisoft Montpellier because this does not feel like a typical Ubisoft release full of padding or busywork. It is focused, dense, and respects your time.  My only real complaint is the main character design. The default look just does not feel like a Persian prince at all and looks out of place to me. It feels more like "Prince of Liberia". Thankfully the Complete Edition includes classic skins inspired by older Prince of Persia titles, which fit the setting much better and made the experience more enjoyable for me.  I originally wrote this game off when I first saw it, but after finally playing it I could barely put it down.  If you like action platformers, Metroidvanias, tough platforming, or stylish combat, this is an easy recommendation. A fantastic surprise and absolutely worth your time.

At the start of my playthrough, I felt as if the map was way too overwhelming and easy to get lost in (in a bad way) which made exploration pretty frustrating. After a few hours though I got used to it and exploration genuinely became fun as opposed to a chore. Platforming is definitely the highlight of this game, movement is super smooth and precise, the levels are meticulously crafted and the attention to detail is remarkable. New platforming mechanics kept the levels fresh and engaging. Boss fights caught me by surprise in this game, they are all different, memorable, and not easy at all. Some fights were a challenge and learning their patterns and how to dodge was insanely fun. The cutscenes looked insanely cool and had a really clean animation, parrying felt very satisfying with a cinematic and immersive cutscene playing on key parries. It genuinely makes your character feel powerful. Overall I had a great time with this game and I definitely recommend it to anyone who likes Metroidvanias with amazing platforming and fun combat.

I went into Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown expecting a graceful parkour adventure with sand, swords, and maybe a dramatic hair flip or two. Instead, what I got was the unexpected joy of repeatedly exiting a boss arena sideways like I’m trying to dodge life choices.  Let’s get one thing straight: this game is gorgeous. The environments? So pretty that I paused mid-sprint just to appreciate the scenery and then immediately regretted it when a monster politely reminded me that beauty waits for no one. Seriously, every background looks like it was painted by someone who also makes motivational posters for cats.  But here’s the twist: the platforming didn’t just work it made me feel like a ninja squirrel with just enough grace and far too much confidence. I leapt, flipped, wall-ran, and occasionally face-planted with the enthusiasm of someone who believes it’ll look cooler in the replay. Nine times out of ten, it did.  Combat is snappy, too. The prince swings his sword with the kind of commitment most of us reserve for opening stubborn snack bags. Every encounter had me thinking, “Surely this one will go smoothly,” followed by a spectacular display of not smoothly  only to laugh and try again. The bosses? Exquisitely extra. One moment you're calmly assessing their attack pattern; the next you’re screaming, “Why is this pillar on fire?!?” at your TV like it betrayed you personally.  The story also refuses to be ordinary. It’s heartfelt in the way only a tale about ancient curses, mysterious crown power, and existential architectural nuisances can be heartfelt. I found myself unexpectedly invested in NPCs who probably think I’m ridiculous but are too polite to say so.  Best of all, the game surprised me at every turn  like when it made perfect platforming feel poetic and makes my repeated failures feel like character building rather than just embarrassment. That’s the kind of emotional roller coaster I didn’t know I signed up for, but definitely wanted to ride.  In conclusion: Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown is beautiful, exhilarating, and refreshingly unpredictable like a yoga class that suddenly becomes a rock concert. Delightful, chaotic, and highly recommended.

Uno de los mejores Prince of Persia si te gustan los juegos de plataformas.  Lo he jugado más de 25 horas, incluyendo el DLC, y puedo decir que me atrapó su ambientación. Las distintas áreas del mapa son lo suficientemente variadas para mantener el juego fresco; la música acompaña muy bien el ritmo y, gráficamente, para ser un 2D es increíble. Los controles son muy fluidos y el sistema de parry me gustó bastante, si no aprendes a usarlo, el juego se complica. Los retos de plataforma tienen una dificultad justa para intentarlos varias veces, aunque algunos pueden resultar excesivos y frustrantes.  Como puntos negativos, los enemigos se vuelven repetitivos tras cierto punto, salvo los jefes, cuyas mecánicas sí se sienten frescas. El DLC no me convenció tanto: aunque la idea de empezar desde cero es interesante, la historia y los mapas no alcanzan el nivel del juego base. Y no esperes una narrativa muy profunda; en ese aspecto se queda bastante meehhh.

Это одна из игр, которую я прошёл на одном дыханье за несколько дней на 100% и планирую второе прохождение на уровне сложности «Бессмертный». Неожиданно. Негаданно. И от кого? «Ubisoft Originals». Браво. Вероятно, студию ждёт банкротство, но напоследок она предложила игрокам очень хорошую игру. Красивый конец. А что же игра?  Признаюсь, поначалу мне не очень понравились драки, поэтому уровень сложности я убавил до минимума. Игра не особо жалеет игрока и не даёт противников под только что освоенные приёмы, чтобы запомнить их. И хотя в игре присутствует тренер в лице Артабана, к которому я, кстати, часто ходил, когда долго не мог пройти испытание, тренер не учит, что во время парирования необходимо левым стиком указать направление, откуда прилетает входящий урон. Мелочь, но из-за неё я на протяжении всего сюжета забыл о парировании, довольно удобной механике. Лирическое отступление. Так вот, после сюжета я стал проходить отдельные испытания и вошёл во вкус местных драк. Собственно, именно поэтому и захотел сыграть на максимальной сложности. Но поначалу из-за банального отсутствия принудительного обучения драки показаись душными. Однако им я поставлю пять из пяти. Они интересные.  Паркур удобный, красивый и минималистичный. Но при игре через контроллер и управлении стиком бывает путаница. Например, думаешь, что жмёшь в сторону, а стик больше смотрит вниз, и Саргон — главный герой — вместо прилепливания к стене летит в бездну. Можно привыкнуть, просто уделите внимание левому большому пальцу. Даже при этом перемещаться очень комфортно и даже, я бы сказал, атмосферно. Саргон очень хорошо и приятно управляется.  Боссы интересные, но некоторые слишком уж быстрые. Наверное, к их скорости можно привыкнуть. Проверю в следующем прохождении. Щадить они вас не будут, а некоторые даже в состоянии на низком уровне сложности убить Саргона, если не улучшено здоровье и мечи. Боссов немного, меньше десяти, но круто проработанные. Один босс из этой игры стоит тридцати из «Elden Ring» по уникальности внешности, движений и типа урона. И, конечно, мотивации, которую не скрывают.  Сюжет с одной стороны обычный, а с другой нет. В целом история про месть, а вот частности весьма специфические. Мультиков в игре немного, но очень красивые. Да, забыл сказать, у боссов есть уникальные кинематографичные анимации, как и у Саргона. Если правильно парировать, можно посмотреть увлекательные добивания, кинематографично обыгранные. Я уж не говорю о том, какие комиксы разворачиваются на битвах с более поздними боссами. Визуальное оформление и открытого мира, и сюжетных вставок великолепны. И отсюда можно перейти к графике.  Графика балансирует между рисованной и просто плохой. Но это очень тонкий, очень удачный баланс, когда игра при виде сбоку не перегружается ненужными элементами и не нагружает комп, а при приближении не заставляет глаза вытекать. Можете смело ставить настройки на пресет ультра или высоко. При виде сбоку, кстати, всё-таки больше походит на рисовку. И в игре много стилизованных под акварельное, наверное, рисование... или пастельное... да, пастельное больше подходит. Короче, картинка радует глаз, как ни крути. Очень мне она понравилась!  В общем, игрушка по моей системе попадает в раздел «Шедеврально», поскольку увлекла меня моментально и надолго. В ней нет недочётов для меня, мне всё в ней понравилось, даже испытания, на которых было сказано немало матерных неологизмов, лесной босс, которую я не смог парировать даже на лёгком уровне сложности, наличие дополнения, которое меня дожидается. Кстати, полная правда о героях игры открывается не в середине. Не-а. Если хотите понять (если купите игру, конечно), почему именно Саргон попал в переплёт, соберите все части мозаики и прочитайте, что получилось. Я бы сказал, «Ubisoft» закинула удочку на будущее франшизы.   Редко попадаются такие игры. Особенно среди современных. «Принц Персии. Утерянная корона» останется со мной навсегда, как когда-то остались «Red Dead Redemption 2» и «Призрак Цусимы». Странный я человек. Всем мира и любви!

我第一次不推荐一个游戏，键盘操作这个跳跳乐直接是灾难，前期玩下来战斗毫无难度，跳跳乐可以让我奔溃

作为去年可能是玩到最大的惊喜，失落王冠确实是一部优秀的银河城游戏。虽然说没有在某些点上特别突出，但是给我最大的感受就是这部作品很扎实。它有着流畅的打击体验，还算出色的BOSS设计，给人留下不错印象的地图设计和能力设计。除了剧情尽管有着一定的一波三折，但是还是没什么存在感以外，实在是很难挑出大的毛病。再加上许多包括截图可以看到去不了的地区是什么情况、在最后给你全搜集的地图在内的小的优化，让人至少在游玩方面还是很舒畅的。总的来说，本作就像一个范本，有着一些让人印象深刻的优点，没什么大问题。 本作无异是结合了不少动作系统以及解密要素的，而同时本作的能力设计也是很好的结合了这一部分。不同于部分银河城如今公式化的勾爪，冲刺，二段跳的能力设计，本作的能力无疑是有意思的，而且还与本作的解谜跳跃元素有着有机的结合。时光回溯，世界切换，飞盘瞬移，抓取等等，都极大的拓展了本作在平台跳跃和解谜上广度与深度。至少有大量机关不会说你第一眼看到就知道是缺少了什么能力，而是后面发现了能力之后恍然大悟想到的。同时本作的锁钥部分做的也出色，印象最深刻的莫过于知识神殿这个关卡。这是一个有着几层锁钥的关卡，其一是本关卡内部解锁的世界切换的能力，从一进入关卡就能看到这个能力的视觉提示，直接对应了这个关卡的上层部分；其二是击败BOSS之后得到的钥匙，直接对应了关卡的底层部分；其三是后续能力带来的一些收藏品这些。整个地图玩起来的先后条理清晰，一层一层层层递进的感觉相当不错。本作地图设计方面比较值得一提的是本作的世界联通感，也就是不同的地区之间的连通性，比如从灯塔一跃而下到达幽灵船，或者是上城区走到古驿道，个人感觉做的还是相当不错的，给人一种世界的可信感。除去最后才给存档点与存档点之间的传送以外让人前面跑图有点火大，其余的基本上都是正向体验。 战斗部分，本作战斗相当流畅舒适。无论是连招还是打击感还是一些打击的动画做的都不错，也给予了详尽（繁琐）的教程。不过可能是因为主角性能太强，导致小怪做的就有点疯狗了，恒沙之坑还有上城这几个地区简直就是粪怪扎堆。BOSS方面设计相对保守，难度都不怎么难，但是打起来感觉还挺爽。但是本作的跳跳乐部分就相对比较折磨了，好几个地图都打的人鬼火冒，比如知识神殿，恒沙之坑的。本作的跳跳乐基本伴随几个特点：距离够长，中间没有或者极少歇点；伴有一定解谜性质，需要观察思考怎么跳，因此导致需要试错；奖励鸡肋。我在相对容易的本体都被搞得有点红温，听说在DLC这些跳跳乐还变本加厉了，那确实不怪玩家破防。 本作对于全收集党还是相当友好的，有拍照和全收集地图还有各个地区的收集要素数量可供参考，这一部分我认为至少值得大部分银河城学习，确实是有效提升玩家游戏体验的好设计。这些照顾玩家体验的设计本作也体现在方方面面，跳过跳跳乐，各种难度的选择，感觉对于游玩体验会是很好的护航。 总的来说，尽管没有特别突出的长板，但凭借着各个方面几乎都没有短板，本作给人的体验还是非常出彩的，确实是一部银河城佳作。

[h2]If you like platformers, Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown feels like it was built by a team of developers who were told “ship something reasonable” and instead responded with a 200-page manifesto on why reasonable is for cowards.[/h2]  I picked it up on a whim because the original Prince of Persia from 1989 was one of my favorite games growing up on an Apple II. I expected a gentle nostalgia pat on the head. A respectful callback. Maybe a weekend fling.  Instead I got ambushed by a game that shows up in a tuxedo, kicks the door in, and says: [b]“We’ve decided to care. A lot.”[/b]  I’m a developer, and this game makes me uncomfortable in the same way a perfectly organized wiring harness does. You admire it. You respect it. You also feel slightly judged by it. The amount of polish here is not normal. The animation, artwork, writing, combat systems, level design… everything feels like it went through 47 iterations and then someone said: [i]“What if we made it better again just to flex?”[/i]  [b]Nothing feels rushed. Nothing feels padded. Nothing feels like it exists because a producer said “we need 40 hours.”[/b] This thing is tighter than a production database five minutes before a major demo.  [h3]Movement and mechanics: actual wizardry[/h3]  Movement feels like your thumbs signed a non-aggression pact with physics. Every jump lands. Every dash behaves. Every recovery feels intentional. You stop thinking about controls and start thinking about possibilities — which is dangerous, because the game has a lot of them.  And the mechanics just keep arriving. Not every few hours. [b]Every 30 minutes.[/b]  Like a magician pulling endless rabbits from a hat, each reveal is more elaborate than the last, just when you think the trick is over.  It never repeats itself. It never goes “[i]okay now do that 900 more times.[/i]” It just keeps escalating like a design team stuck in a positive feedback loop. New traversal ideas. New combat layers. New puzzles. All cleanly integrated. No spaghetti. No jank. Just systems stacked like a well-written codebase that somehow never accrued tech debt.  [h3]Combat: you are required to participate[/h3]  Combat actually expects you to have a pulse. Timing matters. Parries matter. Combos matter. Enemies behave like they attended meetings about their purpose. Boss fights feel like proper duels instead of “large creature with a health bar and unresolved childhood trauma.”  [b]You don’t win by clicking faster. You win by getting better.[/b] Strange concept /s, works beautifully.  [h3]Exploration: a controlled substance[/h3]  Exploration is pure dopamine engineering. The map is basically a controlled substance. I say “[i]one more room[/i]” the way people say “[i]one more episode,[/i]” except the episode is a perfectly designed traversal puzzle and suddenly it’s 2:30 a.m. and I’m explaining to myself why sleep is technically optional.  Every path leads to something useful, clever, or suspiciously well hidden. The game respects your intelligence and then quietly rewards it. Repeatedly. Like a lab rat with a mortgage.  [h3]Developer brain review[/h3]  I’m only about 25% through and my brain keeps blue-screening with the same error: [b]HOW DOES THIS GAME EXIST?[/b]  In an era of battle passes, early-access apologies, and day-one patches the size of Canada, this thing just shows up fully formed like some kind of mythical creature. No bloat. No filler. No visible compromise. Just craft.  The developers are operating on another plane of existence. The gameplay is permanently set to 11, but it’s a clean 11. Studio reference monitor 11. No distortion. Just clarity and power.  I’ve been gaming for 45 years. I’ve seen everything. I am jaded. I am tired. My expectations are low and my standards are unreasonable.  This game still walked in, made eye contact, and overachieved anyway.  [h2]Final verdict[/h2]  It should have been in every Game of the Year conversation, and honestly should have won by a landslide.  If you like tight mechanics, smart design, and games built by people who clearly refused to phone it in, buy it immediately and cancel your evening plans. Possibly your week. Potentially your relationships.  This is what happens when a studio cooks without interference.

Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown é uma delícia!!  Esse jogo mostra a capacidade desperdiçada da Ubisoft e o que acontece quando a coisa mais importante na produção de jogos é o fator diversão, e não o monetário. Os desenvolvedores têm anos de experiência, e isso se mostra no balanceamento de todos os elementos.  A começar por uma das coisas mais importantes em um jogo metroidvania: a movimentação. O Sargon tem um controle extremamente fluido; ele tem o peso certo, a velocidade certa, e andar, pular e correr são responsivos em todos os momentos. Os upgrades de movimentação são bastante criativos, e a forma como eles são usados nas seções de plataforma e nas lutas é incrível principalmente o teleporte. Que habilidade maravilhosa! Você pode criar um ponto de teleporte no meio da luta e, quando o chefe vai para outra área, você simplesmente teleporta para atrás dele e continua batendo. Você se sente muito OP.  Outro ponto forte é a distribuição dos upgrades. Você vai ganhando habilidades do início ao fim do jogo, e isso é ótimo, porque sempre temos novos pontos para visitar com as novas habilidades. O backtracking poderia ser cansativo, mas não é, principalmente por causa da habilidade final de teleportar diretamente para as árvores, em vez de usar apenas os pontos de teleporte padrão (“cavalo”). Isso é uma decisão de design que claramente mostra experiência.  A história existe e é legal, com referências históricas interessantes porém eu sou um chimpanzé quando o assunto é esse período histórico, não sei nada. O que realmente me chama atenção é a direção de arte: que jogo bonito! Ele faz um uso excelente das cores, tudo é muito vivo, e cada área é linda dentro da sua própria temática.  Por fim, preciso elogiar o combate. Ele lembra muito um hack and slash 2D. Temos combos, ataques aéreos e, dependendo da direção do analógico, golpes diferentes. As boss fights acompanham isso, e todas são maravilhosas, sem ressalvas todas mesmo.  Infelizmente, eu queria mais das side quests. Fiz todas para a platina do jogo base (a DLC ainda não comprei). Algumas são realmente legais, como ir atrás dos oito discípulos e enfrentar um mini-chefe no final, ou coletar todas as Areias do Tempo. Mas outras, como a do coletor de luas, eu achei só chata. A do pirata é tranquila, um bom desafio de plataforma. Já a do alquimista, junto com a das Areias do Tempo, são as mais longas e poderiam ter recompensas melhores, como uma boss fight secreta.  O ápice do jogo, pra mim, está no elemento de plataforma. Sem exceção, todos os desafios são maravilhosos. A movimentação fluida, as habilidades de teleporte, chicote, dash aéreo e viagem entre dimensões são exploradas ao máximo. As animações do Sargon fazem você realmente sentir que está praticando parkour.  De verdade, esse jogo fica pra mim no top 3 de melhores metroidvanias, sendo mais divertido que Silksong, sendo bem sincero.

Grałem w wiele gier z gatunku Mertoidvania i ta jest mid. Daleko jej do perełek takich jak Hollow Knight czy Blasphemous. Ale jako platformówka jest całkiem spoko. Czy polecam? Dla fanów platformówek i łatwych metroidvani tak. Ogólna ocena 6/10 -Mapa 8/10 (duża, różnorodna) -Muzyka 5/10 (mid kompletnie) -Grafika 6,5/10 (ogólnie lepsza niż mid ale widać czasami kiepskie tekstury co psuje imersję) -Platformówka 8,5/10 (bardzo dobre elementy platformowe ale trochę ich dużo) -Metroidvania 7/10 (ma wymagane elementy, nawet niespotkane w innych grach ale ogół gatunku zbyt platformowa) -Bossy 6/10 (mało ich, ogólnie są dość dobrze zrobione ale brak dobrej muzyki psuje wiele z nich, fazy różne są i ogólnie niektóre walki są nawet spoko reszta mid) -Przeciwnicy 7/10 (chyba każda część mapy ma swoich własnych przeciwników ogólnie spoko) -Rozwój 6/10 (jest, da się ulepszać ekwipunek, ale ogólnie to kiepsko rozwiązany jest ten system ulepszeń, amulety są przydatne ale zupełnie standardowe, te mniej standardowe są mało przydatne) -NPC 3/10 (bardzo słabi NPC, nudni, bez wyrazu, bez fabuły) -Questy 5/10 (mid, dobrze że w ogóle są jakieś) -Walka 7/10 (całkiem git, satysfakcjonujące kombinacje można robić) -Fabuła 5,5/10 (mid, da się poczytać o świecie gry w grze i zrozumieć co się dzieje ale dość późno, zdarzają się niedopowiedzenia, ogólnie mi sie średnio podobała) -Błędy 4/10 (niestety występowały błędy gameplayowe dość znaczące najpewniej przez jeden z nich nie mogę zdobyc osiągnięcia na max HP, wizualne też się zdarzały, ze sterowaniem nawet często) -Trudność 7/10 (można customizować, ale w tym gatunku nie powinno się dać według mnie ale jeśli ktoś chce łatwiejszej wersji to może sobie ułatwić)

Просто ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ игра!  Задушился очень сильно крупными ААА проектами за последнее время, и что-то особо ничего не цепляло. Еще сыграло роль то, что последние месяцы проходил как раз игры от юбиков -- мифическую трилогию Assassin's creed, Вальгалла кстати необъективно захейченая игра, и shadows буквально на днях. После пресного, душного, не цепляющего Shadows Принц Персии от тех же убиков зашла на ура. На удивление сюжет цепляет (не знаю почему многие ноют о сюжете, в игре он правда классный, изучать лор интересно, мифология как никак). Геймплей -- просто шикарен, не было ни одного момента в игре, где геймплей меня мог надоесть. Боссов проходил на максимальной сложности без особых проблем, а вот на платформинге мог застрять по часу в одной локации. И даже после этого я не чувствовал какое то отторжение, нежелание играть дальше и так далее. Игра тебе бросает довольно сложный челлендж, с сомнительной наградой часто, но сам процесс прохождения этого челленджа настолько кайфовый, что потраченного времени не жаль. Разнообразие дэшей, прыжков, телепортов и прочего ничуть не казуалит игру, а наоборот добавляет глубины, позволяет возвращаться в ранее исследованные части карты и доставляет массу удовольствия. Если коротко -- юбики вспомнили, что игры должны быть фановыми, при этом не превращаться в клоунаду. За такую цену -- советую всем брать

A very interesting metroidvania.  The Lost Crown, like many modern metroidvanias, includes soulslike elements. I'm not particularly a fan of those in general, but I mostly like how it's done in this game. The combat feels very acrobatic and spectacular, reminding me of Metroid Dread, especially the bosses. I do believe there's a slight problem in balancing, as the random enemies you find in the map are maybe harder than the bosses. Difficulty is generally very customizable in this game, but I found myself not wanting to increase the difficulty for bosses in case I made the world enemies too hard. I also don't like how enemies respawn even if you don't rest at a control point, but on the other hand there are no corpse runs so I'll gladly take it.  The level design is awesome. The map is very big but doesn't feel repetitive thanks to some excellent art. Some areas are truly unique. There's a good dose of hard platforming, which I love. I don't know if there is potential for sequence breaking, but in any case the game lets you get lost, so the exploration is rewarding.  I did not like the first few hours, though. It takes too long to get into the main map, and the first hour is heavy with dialogue and cutscenes. It gets much better once you start getting a few abilities and unlocking the game's potential.  In summary, a great metroidvania that everyone interested in the genre should try.

Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown punches well above its weight.   A stylish art direction and vibrant visuals give it a high production feel rarely seen in the genre, while the music perfectly supports the adventure, ramping up when story demands it.   Gameplay is crisp, responsive, and deeply satisfying, making both combat and platforming incredibly engaging. Time powers are distributes evenly throughout the missions as well which keeps you on your toes as you always have to level your skill up to deal with newer mobs.  Add in an interesting, albeit imperfect story that keeps things moving, and you get a complete experience that puts The Lost Crown head-to-head with games like Metroid Dread and Symphony of the Night.

Juego bastante bueno y bien pulido. En mi humilde opinión, es de lo poquísimo rescatable que ha sacado Ubisoft en los últimos años.  La historia es algo simplona y los personajes tienden a ser bastante arquetípicos. Por no hablar de que hay huecos argumentales y cosas mal explicadas en los documentos...  El apartado artístico pudo haber sido más detallado, pero funciona. Los efectos de luces, flashes y colores sí que me parecen resultones.  El combate a dos espadas resulta ser tremendamente ágil y satisfactorio. Hay posibilidad de parry y remate especial a los enemigos. Hay más variedad de combos de lo que parecía en un principio.  El diseño de niveles está perfectamente cohesionado y se va abriendo poco a poco sin abrumar. Se agradece la posibilidad de hacer capturas de pantalla y ponerlas en el mapa para acordarse después al hacer backtracking. Se pueden equipar y mejorar amuletos.  Encontramos las dosis perfectas de plataformeo, sortear trampas con pinchos y puzzles, recoger coleccionables y adquirir habilidades únicas como el clon de sombra.  La dificultad en general es algo condescendiente, a excepción de algunos bosses o retos opcionales con tiempo límite de plataformeo con trampas. Con todo esto en mente, se puede recomendar como iniciación a los metroidvania.  Como únicas pegas: los huecos argumentales y la corta duración.

Юбики вспомнили, что игры должны быть веселыми и интересными! Отличная и очень красивая игра. Показатель того, что можно делать, когда за этот жанр берутся крупные компании с ресурсами. Вначале мне показалось, что игра слишком простая, первый босс дался довольно легко. Но сложность прогрессирует весьма равномерно. В итоге игра предлагает интересную, в меру сложную игру для тех, кто не ищет челенджа. А для тех, кто ищет - челенджа будет предостаточно. Боссфайты интересные, но не скажу, что сильно впечатлили. Было интереснее потом проходить их без урона, или, по крайней мере, пытаться. Головоломок не много, что есть весьма интересные. А одну опциональную даж не смог пройти, в итоге забил. Но самый сок здесь - это платформинг. Он сделан настолько интересно и сложно, что не отпускает. Некоторые уровни вообще будто из супермитбоя. Шикарная игра.

Metroidvania di grande qualità che merita il pollice blu, ricorda Guacamelee! sia nelle sfide platform che nel combattimento. Le parti di platforming sono di una precisione clamorosa e danno soddisfazione anche nelle fasi più hardcore, con dei puzzle ambientali ben riusciti. il combattimento è veloce e bilanciato, basato su parate e combo di attacchi base molto intuitive, difficili da masterare ma a normale non serve essere skillati, invece se lo trovate facile si può alzare il livello ben 2 volte. L'esplorazione della grande mappa è una goduria, con una marea di indicatori ad uso illimitato e alcuni limitati che fanno lo screenshot al punto di nostro interesse, ci sono tanti ceckpoint ma pochi viaggi rapidi. Mai punitivo, mai frustrante, mai troppo facile ... bel gioco, bravi, grazie. P.S. !! - ricordatevi di caricare manualmente i salvataggi nel cloud dal menu del gioco, capita che si buggano, non riconosce più quelli in locale e spariscono, ho dovuto ricominciare a un passo dalla fine, vabbè è uguale tanto è bello un bel pò - !!

Good game, but not great. Platforming puzzles are awesome and often include wild mechanics combinations. Gaining new abilities greatly increases movement possibilities and the controls feel very good. Combat is also very engaging, learning enemies attack patterns is mandatory as parrying attacks heavily pays off, mostly in boss fights. Bosses seemed to me less challenging as the game got through. The story and the characters, nevertheless, seemed shallow to me. There are a lot of characters, but most of them do not have a strong identity or contribute significantly to the development. The end left me with a poor sense of completion. Good game to spend time. Good platforming, good combat, good exploration. Not gonna blow your head though.

Prince of Persia The Lost Crown surprised me.  As a fan of the older POP games I was surprised that this entry is a metroidvania.  I started playing it and got quickly hooked.  It's exploration, platform, combat loop is very well made, and I especially enjoyed the boss fights.  The story was very safe, but at no point was a flaw...  Just predictable and comforting.  After finishing the game in about 35 hours, I can definitely recommend this game if you like metroidvania, 2D platformer, or action games.  Pros: + The gameplay loop is very well made. + The boss fights were a highlight and I especially loved the creative use of the character's abilities. + The combat/combo debt.  It's not DMC 5, but it's very strong for this type of games. + Solid character/ability progression. + The platforming challenges can be quite challenging. + Ran smoothly.  0 crash or technical issue on my end. + A True Warrior... Needs No Weapon.  Cons: - Some of the mob encounters were very serviceable.  Halfway through the game, I found myself avoiding them completely and being focused on exploration, platforming, or getting to the next boss.    Verdict: 9.0/10.

波斯王子：失落的王冠全成就纪念！ 这款类银的画面制作相当精美！而且文化气息也很浓厚，虽然部分跳跳乐难度较高恶意较大，但总体而言瑕不掩瑜，我很喜欢 下面是一些印象比较深的点： 1.支线任务-难于登天： 这个任务非常搞心态而且卡了两个成就，如果不追求全成就的话非常建议跳过！ 它的要求是在一大堆上下长有尖刺的移动柱子之间一口气收集三颗铃铛并带回来，中间穿插了许多需要使用鼠标瞄准的钩索才能到达的点，一旦爬柱子时失误撞到了刺就会前功尽弃，只好从头开始收集 对我来说最难的部分就是钩索了。因为我是一名纯键盘玩家，非常不擅长用鼠标来瞄准钩索。我的解决办法是把鼠标固定在屏幕正中央，这样人物跳起来的时候鼠标在他头顶上方一点点，然后把钩索的识别范围开到最大，这样相当于实现了小范围的自动瞄准，尝试了一个多小时后终于成功！  2.主角的能力 这款游戏的主角移动能力相当新奇！最有意思的点就是他可以记录位置并在原地留下一个影子，再按一次按键会直接时光倒流回记录的时间点。游戏的很多谜题都是基于此设置的，我觉得如果这个能力在战斗中活用的话会非常精彩！可惜我没有善用空间系能力的手（） 这个能力还对我搞oc起到了很大的帮助嘿嘿嘿，夏夜的传送门原来也可以这样用  3.师傅 这个游戏里有一个可以训练你的师傅，完成了他的全部训练后就可以和他切磋。很有意思的点是他的特殊攻击，是拍了一下主角的屁股，好好笑。然后主角的所有血变成了九日那样的内伤，就是接下来不受伤的话可以慢慢恢复但受了伤就会全扣光，吓哭了 但我很喜欢这种切磋的氛围！没有生死决斗，只有相互之间的鼓励和调侃，我喜欢这样的感觉  4.DLC 这个游戏的DLC更是重量级，体量不小而且跳跳乐难度颇高。我跳了好久。不过正常流程里的跳跳乐可以在设置里直接跳过，所以应该还好？  5.CG动画 这个单独拿出来说是因为这个游戏的所有小怪和boss都有独特cg动画，一般是在战斗中特殊格挡之后触发。我很喜欢这个设置，这真是小巧思！再结合前面说的这么丰厚的文化和宗教氛围，感觉当成画质更高的渎神代餐来玩也没问题了！  说到小巧思，这个游戏是有宝箱怪的，而且被咬住了也有CG动画（）而且死了还会爆炸，这下又有tc味了，小巧思！你这家伙  总体而言我还是很喜欢这款游戏的，制作很精美，难度可以自由选择，打完之后感觉很畅快，我喜欢！

[h1] Персидский принц чинит время. Снова! [/h1]  В целом крепкая метроидвания. После Силксонга ощущается немного простоватой и короткой, но хуже её это не делает.  Принц, который не принц, [spoiler] но вообще принц [/spoiler] , следуя за похитителями своего сюзерена попадает во временную аномалию.    Для жанра тут всё стандартно. Исследуем открытый мир, находим улучшалки, которые помогают нам бить врагов, быстрее перемещаться по локациям и находить секретки.  Игра вообще очень комфортная. Каждые несколько сюжетных квестов нам дают карты сокровищ, на которые отмечены все секретки. Мы внутри игры можем делать как отметки, так и скриншоты, чтобы понимать, что именно нам помешало попасть в то или иное место. В игре отсутствует контактный урон. Я слышал, что в Жукорыцаре это многих раздражало.  Боевка немного напоминает файтинг, но враги достаточно простые и крутить комбухи особо смысла нет. Может на высоких сложностях и надо, но лично я обошелся и без них.  Атмосфера Персии выдержана отлично и подана без "исторической достоверности" со стороны Юбисофт. Эта игра вообще ощущается как старая игра юбиков из нулевых и начала десятых. Красивая и емкая. В духи старого Принца Персии или Beyond good and evil. Аномалия просто. Жаль, что контора Гиймо опять всё пролюбила. Может через несколько лет мы всё-таки снова попадем в древнюю Персию

Долго откладывал покупку. Отчасти не хотел поддерживать Юбисофт, отчасти - стоимость игры приличная для нишевого жанра. Обычно подобные игры стоят дешевле. Но на новогодней распродаже не удержался и кинул тысчонку в монитор.  Сходу игра рисует красивую графику, демонстрируя масштабную битву с кушанами на подступах к столице Персии. А дальше огромная карта горы Каф, на территории которой стоит величественный дворец шаха Дария с залами, подземельями, обсерваторией, тюрьмой, библиотекой и другими помещениями. Есть и секретные проходы, и загадки на логику, и большие сегменты акробатической практики.   Что подкупило, авторы игры всегда вознаграждают игрока за действия. Прошёл босса - обязательно дадут новую способность, видишь много шипов и пил - значит в конце тебя ждёт монета, сундук или урна с песком. Что-то пропустил, можешь купить карту со всеми отмеченными секретами, не хочешь покупать - возьми амулет, который будет подсказывать про секретные проходы. Потерялся и не знаешь куда идти - можешь за недорого взять подсказку у местного персонажа Фарибы. С новыми способностями открываются и комбо-удары, с которыми нас познакомит член отряда "Бессмертных" Артабан.  У него же можно попрактиковаться и довести ближний бой до автоматизма, заодно дадут сразиться с боссами, которых встретим в течение игры. Авторы порционно добавляют новые механики, благодаря чему я всегда ощущал прогрессию и не чувствовал, что герой не развивается. Да и грамотное чередование боевых сегментов с паркурными не даёт игре погрузиться в рутинность. Разработчики прекрасно понимают, что такое баланс вовлечённости.  Не к чему подкопаться, всё сделано на достойном уровне. Карта подробная с адекватной навигацией, затемнения на ней всегда напомнят, где ты не был. Есть возможность делать внутренние скриншоты, и есть набор меток на все случаи жизни. На карте автоматически будут отображаться побочные задания, которые будет брать главный герой.  Герой чётко отзывается на команды и не ощущается тяжеловесным. Ловко прыгает от стен, делает двойные прыжки и скользит в воздухе, успевая цепляться за перекладины и уступы, ловко лавируя между смертоносными ловушками. Сражаться с противниками тоже интересно, помимо парных клинков есть магические приёмы, дарованные богом Симургом, а также лук с шакрамом для дальних атак. В качестве местных перков выступают амулеты с разными свойствами, которые можно улучшать у кузнеца. Ячейки под амулеты можно расширять, что позволит одеть достаточно большое количество подвесок, тем самым создавая разные билды для героя.  Достойная метройдвания. Изысканное украшение в библиотеке магазина. Выглядит дорого-богато, играется просто отлично. Есть реиграбельность с режимами без смертей и на время. На деке работает хорошо, никаких тормозов и вылетов, правда лучше не переходить в Юбисофт Коннект, который сильно грузит деку, да и интерфейс приложения не заточен под маленький экран.

Dans l'ensemble PoP est un bon jeu, j'ai passé un bon moment. Pour la durée de vie en explorant bien sans faire du 100% je suis à 24h, soit une journée complète.   Ce jeu prend sa place dans le genre qu'il représente. On y trouve beaucoup de bonnes idées, dans les mécaniques de déplacement, dans la variété de biome qui sont en adéquation avec l'univers. Dans l'ensemble il y a une certaine logique et nous ne sommes pas vraiment perdu.   J'ai en revanche trouvé un petit manque d'équilibrage sur certains aspects. Notamment sur les combats de boss. Les patterns sont peu lisibles et amènent souvent à prendre des dégâts, en revanche c'est loin d'être punitif et le challenge est réussi en trois essais maximum. Ce qui donne une impression de combat gagné sans avoir bien compris ni géré convenablement ce qui nous est demandé. On ne ressent pas vraiment de fierté à réussir ces étapes.   À l'inverse, les phases de plateformes sont elles bien corsées. Surtout passé un certain seuil dans le jeu. Elles demanderont un peu de concentration, dont certaines optionnelles qui donnent un vrai challenge.   Globalement l'exploration se fait bien, on prend du plaisir à débloquer des passages qui nous faisaient barrages auparavant.   +++ - L'exploration, - Les différents pouvoirs, - L'univers, - L'OST, - Les phases de plateformes, - Les défis secondaires.   Pour les choses à améliorer :  - Quelques curseurs à régler (jeu casu, qui ne l'est pas, avec pleins d'indices mais des phases de plateformes hard pour récupérer des items complétement optionnels), - Des combats qui pourraient être plus simple dans l'exécution mais plus punitifs, - Des quêtes secondaires qui pousseraient plus à l'exploration et à l'interaction entre les différents acteurs de cet univers.

Absolute banger.  The traversal, movement, and combat were all top notch.  I wasn't into the art of the characters, but the scenery/backdrops were awesome.  Give it a go if you're into metroidvania's.  It does it very well.

Great game.  Combat was well-done; has good flow once you got the hang of it, lots of combos and customization options in the form of equippable amulets. Particularly enjoyed the bosses.  Platforming felt good and was fun. Tons of challenges throughout the world to overcome for rewards. Most new abilities you unlock meaningfully change how you move through the world, adding to your movement as well as seeing use in combat.  Lots of collectibles to find and places to explore, and as you progress you constantly open up shortcuts that make future backtracking much easier.  Writing was a bit weak, but nothing egregiously bad. There are also optional Divine Challenges that are often just brutally difficult and frustrating (particularly the combat challenges); if I were to replay the game I don't think I'd do them again.  I'd say the game is generally on the more difficult side, but there are a number of difficulty and accessibility options to tweak the challenge to your own liking.  Overall, one of the top metroidvanias I've played. Definitely recommend.

Приятная графика, игровой мир. Любопытное переосмысление сюжетной линии и способностей времени. Есть интересные способности и головоломки. Открывать мир любопытно. В игре челленджевые паркуры и некоторые арены. Если слишком сложно - можно подкрутить настройки и пройти всё на изи, за что спасибо. Когда после 10го раза надоедает пилить одних и тех же врагов - врубаешь внутренние читы и проходишь этот кошмар. Эти самые "испытания" действительно то еще испытание...  Из минусов назову обширные пустые локации с неким фиш аем, который при паркуре на большой дистанции - напрягает. Очень много внутреннего лора вшито в огромные простынки-записки. Читать тяжело. Особенно хочу отметить юбисофт порт и постояные вылеты после подбора записки с большим текстом. Игра намертво виснет, а сохраняется только на контрольных точках... если прошел секцию и получил записку, а потом словил крэш - перепроходить секцию душновато. ОСОБЕННО это было на испытании отшельника. Никогда я так исльно не боялся получить записку лора по игре. После где-то 8го вылета из-за записки даже хотелось бы поставить негативную оценку, но чисто за это хаять игру не буду.

Great combat with some genuinely tricky and rewarding boss fights (you'll rapidly learn that the pattern is "Oh, this isn't so bad", then a quick cutscene where the boss goes Super Saiyan and then you die), thoughtful puzzles and some of the most enjoyable precision platforming challenges I've ever played.   All of this is embedded in a visually gorgeous map with a great understanding of Metroidvania structure: meaningful and mechanically rewarding shortcuts and backtracks and enemies that get recontextualized by your kit upgrades as you revisit areas -- while also mostly avoiding several MV tropes I'm not such a fan of, particularly something I suspected *might* be implemented here but thankfully isn't. That is, hiding a true ending, or worse, a large portion of the game behind completion of what are apparently optional side objectives.   Overall, a subtle thing I appreciated here that the game is honest and communicates with you effectively. By this I mean things like how it ensures you'll understand what all your abilities can and can't do as soon as you get them from a combination of useful tutorial text and the practical scenarios it puts you in shortly after obtaining them, how secret breakable walls chime and flash after hitting them for the first time, and when you see a path that requires abilities you don't have yet to make progress, it's always pretty clear that proceeding that way is hopeless after at most a couple tries. Sometimes games like this can make you feel like you're being tricked rather than challenged, and I never got that from The Lost Crown.   The writing isn't anything too exceptional, and in fact in a few places feels like characters go missing unsatisfyingly or that whole arcs had to be cut that were intended flesh out what were initially signposted as themes or narrative motifs. However, at least for me, this isn't too big a deal. I'll always take a mechanically excellent game with a lackluster story over the reverse case, and even when The Lost Crown is doing narrative stuff, the cutscenes look pretty and don't ever overstay their welcome, the ambiance and aesthetic of the setting is generally excellent, and the ability to play the game dubbed in Farsi with English subtitles helped sell the charm of experiencing an adventure in a faraway place.   This game was a gift, but I happily would have paid full price for it having finished it up this afternoon, and will likely check out the DLC they shipped as well. Strongly recommend.

This game’s quality surprised me, especially considering it’s a Ubisoft game. The gameplay is fluid, responsive, and really enjoyable. The backtracking and exploration in this game are fun, rewarding, and never feel boring. The game keeps adding new mechanics as you progress, which helps keep things fresh. The environments are well designed and varied, and the story is surprisingly good and interesting.  Most Metroidvania games tend to be too long and can feel like a chore if you want to complete everything. The Lost Crown, however, strikes a great balance, it’s not too long and not too short. It took me 28 hours to complete everything in the base game and the DLC.  The best part is that it still feels like a true Prince of Persia game and didn’t lose its identity. I hope Ubisoft makes more games like this instead of the copy and paste titles they’ve been releasing.

As a lover of the very original Prince of Persia on the Amiga back in time, I think this is an absolutely brilliant game and a top tier reimagining of that classic side scrolling PoP experience. Exploration is fun, the combat is simple yet satisfying and the platforming/puzzling is perfect. It does get quite challenging in parts, but the upgrades available make most of the combat a breeze eventually and it adds a sense of purpose to the mobs combat as you farm for XP towards upgrades - but it never feels like a grind. The sense of progression feels good. You unlock game changing abilities as you progress the main story which in turn encourages back tracking to reach previously inaccessible platforms/rooms for items, and some sections require some real thinking to figure out - I love that.  The visuals and performance standout. The art direction is great and the graphics are very impressive on the OLED steam deck.  I am very impressed by the scale of the game. The map is a lot larger than I expected going into this, and 14 hours in, it's just getting bigger.  Very impressed.

A surprisingly competent metroidvania! That comes with the usual: A huge map loaded with upgrades, hidden walls and mini-bosses. Challenging platforming sections that pushes its slick movement to its limits, and satisfying combat with juicy parries, smooth dodges and abilities that are straight up dope as hell. Couple this with quality of life fixes like screenshots on the map to remember where you got stuck and treasure maps to highlight pickups you might've missed, this metroidvania is pretty much a must-play for any fan of the genre.

A really amazing Metroidvania game.  I like the Prince of Persia series and I like Metroidvania games, so I did think I would enjoy this, but I didn't expect to enjoy it quite as much as I did. Especially with it being an Ubisoft game. I mean, it has been a long time since I've played a new Ubisoft game that I really enjoyed. Of course not long after this came out Ubisoft in their infinite wisdom decided this game didn't sell enough and shut down the studio and the possibility of a sequel with it. But I digress...  The game has a really great story. It isn't exactly original, but it's just really well told. It's a bit like if Prince of Persia was a superhero movie, with these cool dramatic face offs and magical setting and powers. It was all just a lot fun. The game has really great 3D graphics for a Metroidvania and seamlessly switches between 3D and 2D perspective, which really enhances the experience. It is fully voice acted throughout as well to a high standard.  The combat is really good, with a good amount of options and depth and abilities to use. There is a decent challenge without being too much. Similar could be said of the platforming and Metroidvanian exploration and map design. There is also great quality of life features,  I loved the feature to screenshot a location of interest and pin it to your map.  The game ran flawlessly with a rock solid 144fps throughout for me. It is definitely worth the asking price too, especially on sale. I mean, I really just don't have any significant criticisms to make of the game, I'm not saying it is the best game ever made, but it is just really great by pretty much any measure. Only bad thing I can say is the use of Ubisoft Connect being mandatory and that Ubisoft did what they did to the dev studio. But the devs definitely did a good job and this definitely deserves a positive review from me.

Overall great 2.5D metroid-vania with tight combat, amazing platforming, beautiful backdrops and fantastic full 3D anime-esque cutscenes/interactions that are all fully voiced. All the praise gameplay-wise. If there's any reason this couldn't compete with something like Hollow Knight, it'd be: IMO, that this game has an uninteresting story and the world and majority of enemies aren't aesthetically fantastical/mystical/flashy enough. Really unfortunate that this game didn't get more attention because of it's originally high $50 USD starting price and backlash on the main character's design...  Other Notes: -Strongly recommend you actively check out combat tutorials given by the your Immortal friend, Artaban, in the Haven.  -Introduces a fantastic quality-of-life where you can take screenshots of places and items you currently don't have access to and plug them right into your map as a reminder for yourself. -Because it's 2.5D the backgrounds look great but the foreground walking area, which is where your focus is most of the time, tends to feel flatter than I'd like. -Didn't like the aesthetic of the user interface/menus. -REALLY needs HDR, the effects and highlights are screaming for it...

Requested a refund after an hour of gameplay.  The worst problem is of course Ubisoft launcher, but that I would overlook if the game would be truly good. It is not. (Also why does the launcher need to know my age if steam already knows it? So suspicious.)  Controls: the LT+x attack worked only like 40% of the time for me, and I can't be bothered to find what is the issue. Generally controller had some odd delays. Parry system is odd because I would expect that parry should be activated as soon as the yellow glow appears, but nope, only when the yellow glow is there AND the enemy is about to strike. Why have the yellow glow then? Confusing. Writing and plot: writing is really cringy and feels both devoid of tone and at the same time trying to sound epic, but actually sounding somehow juvenile.  There is a lot of dialog, but it feels pointles and repetative. I got it, we need to slay the general, you could say it once. I got it that we gotta go save the prince if that prince was just kidnapped in front of me. Feels like every objective is repeated trice for the people who skip dialog and cutscenes. Disappointing, I expected it to be story driven - and it sort of is, but the writing truly lets it down. Game progression: on "normal" there are odd difficulty spikes, will not say where because spoilers.  At the same time regular enemies feel boring and grindy. Artstyle: screenshots look better then the actual game because the game is too flashy for my taste. I really dislike special effects that obscure the character and cartoonish introduction of bosses. Also something about the animation feels plastic-y. I see how the game goes for a distinct style, I just really dislike this style. It is as if it tries to appeal to a teenage boy who grew up reading comics? I can't quite put my finger on it. Music: is just generic. All in all I think my problem is my high expectations from both Prince of Persia franchise and from metroidvania as a genre.

Ubisoft kann noch Spiele machen und das sieht man an PoP. Besser kann man ein Metroidvania nicht machen. Tolle Story, tolle Grafik, tolle Vertonung und Sound und super Steuerung. Progress in dem Spiel ist echt toll gemacht, die Fähigkeiten finde ich super. DIe Länge des Spiels für mich perfekt mit ca. 20h für die Main Quest.   Für mich auch DEUTLICH besser als Hollow Knight oder Silksong.  Die negativen Kommentare für das Spiel sind alles andere als berechtigt: - Nutzt Ubisoft Launcher, wie jedes Ubisoft Game, das macht das Spiel nicht schlecht - Spielprogression Verluste: Konnte ich nie nachvollziehen. Gehe zu einem Baum und dein Spiel wird gespeichert (nicht zu einer Teleportationsstatue). Hat bei mir IMMER funktioniert. - Spiel stürzt ab: Ich spiel es auf einem Potato-PC (Ryzon 2700X, RTX 2070, Windows 10), kein einziges Mal abgestürzt. Es gab nur einmal ein Bug, da war ich unsterblich, habe ich aber erst bei einem Boss gemerkt und ging beim Laden / Baum wieder weg. - Quicktravel ist unfair: Nein ist es nicht, später kann man zwischen den Bäumen Quicktraveln, dann ist es sogar zu trivial.

My first impression of this game was it looked like a mobile game in some ways... So not a game I loved first looking at it. But after seeing only positive things and an 87 on Metacritic I got it on sale. I was recently done completing some metroidvanias  like The Messenger, Guacamelee!, and Ender Lilies; and the Lost Crown really surprised me! The overall depth of this game is remarkable for the type of game. It has deep movement depth that you can get really good at with practice. I never mastered it but could see the potential if you were a little smarter and better than me. Same thing with the combat... The Depth. You can juggle enemies and have a wide range of combos to pull off. It has the coolest feature I've seen in a metroidvania to keep track of important spots you need to come back to. They thought of it all and UbiSoft is absolutely trending upward for me with this surprising hit!

The Lost Crown is a very enjoyable 'vania that had much sharper teeth than I expected. And that is kind of a double-edged sword.  I really liked the parry heavy combat (parrying isn't a requirement to master but not using the mechanic would make combat rather dull) but the precision 2d platforming often got frustrating. It really makes you appreciate the design of games like Celeste which is arguable harder but less frustrating due to fair and amazing level design and tight controls. It wasn't frustrating enough to make me shelve the game but I'm not going for 100% or finishing the DLC. Boss battles were good fun!  The art direction and music are very nice and some of the zones had unique gimmicks. The story and characters didn't grab me at first but I was into it at the end and think it wrapped up satisfyingly.  I really hope Ubisoft keep the 2d Prince alive (and their AA divisions which have definitely made much more enjoyable games than their AAA dept.) There's a lot of good in this game and I would definitely be into playing another game from this team!

A great game like Hallow Knight but with expanded production values. Bright anime inspired presentation, tight platforming, and deep combat akin to a fighting game combine for a top notch game that stands out from many Metrovania games. The transversal abilities not only aid in map access and puzzles but also helps in combat. Full voice acting in many languages including Persian, a franchise first! The many quality of life improvements makes this game the ideal introduction to the Metroidvania genre.  The main negative would be the always online requirement using Ubisoft's app. It is maddening to stay online for a single player game when a Ubisoft account is not required for the Switch version. In contrast to the multilayered gameplay and presentation is the relatively safe story. I suspect future content would take more narrative risks but the dev team was disbanded shortly after the game release. Outside of Metriod and Castlevania games I don't think gamers are willing to pay Ubisoft's full asking price for a 2D game.  Sadly Ubisoft's reputation and choice on The Lost Crowns pricing and steam release handicapped the game's ability to showcase itself to the general public. While unconnected to other Prince of Persia games this version is the best modern game in the franchise.  Definitely worth getting especially on sale which Ubisoft games tend to do.

Full disclosure, I've now beaten this twice. So not recommending may seem odd.   First playthrough was via Ubisoft connect. Game was fun but SO MANY issues with connect. Constantly would get booted for losing access to the server. Doubly wild as my internet would be fine and it's a single player game.  On release there were a lot of bugs. As well as AI content that was left in the game on accident. But I perservered, nonetheless and enjoyed the game.   Recently bought here on steam and, while the performance on deck is great, the bugs it now has are WILD.  So many soft locks where I can't slide under small doors, full size doors have invisible walls, traps are frozen (so they block your path/ progress), player damage just disabled itself at one point, the first main boss fight has a big door open after you win....it was open during the fight so I could just walk away. But I'm sure that would have broken something. Lots of other little bugs like sounds just stopping, etc.   Had to quit, often, and eventually had to reinstall to get past some soft locks.   Finally beat it. Then went on to play the DLC and it's so incredibly unfun. It's so difficult that if you played when it's available, you'd hate the sudden difficulty spike, but if you wait till after you finish the game you'll be annoyed that all the movements and powers you've learned are just gone. Honestly a pretty awful design choice.  Most of the challenge comes from extreme traps and high aggro enemies. Lots of hits or damage you couldn't have foreseen and thus is unavoidable.  Would love to see more from this team. But the QC has got to... Well, freshly, exist for starters.

Коротке враження про гру:  PoP: The Lost Crown - це чуть не еталон жанру метроідваній.   Коли я бачу, що гру видає, чи тим більше розробляє Ubisoft, у мене одразу падають сподівання на хорошу гру. Але ця гра стала виключенням. Не знаю, хто там зібрався в цій студії з Монпел'є, але цієї студії вже немає, бо для Ubisoft випуск хороших ігор заборонений.   У грі хороше мабуть все, починаючи від музики та візуального стилю, закінчуючи локаціями та прокачкою персонажа. Та тут навіть можна зробити скриншот локації і він буде показуватись на карті локації, щоб нагадати гравцю, що саме там було. А ця механіка була чимось новим для жанру.   Боси - топ, платформінг, як і належне серії - топ, бої - топ. Ця гра на рівні Silksong, якщо в чомусь не краще. І це може підтвердити будь-який поціновувач жанру.   Якщо ви любите метроідванії, то не пограти в The Lost Crown - величезна помилка. Це вам не рудиментарний Metroid, у який просто боляче грати. Це гра, яка запам'ятається і через декілька років буде вважатись класикою.

Prince of Persia The Lost Crown is a standout metroidvania in a genre that I think can easily feel unoriginal and same-y. While it does have some familiar navigation powers like double jump and dash, the game also introduces a few "time" powers that really add a fun, cool layer to the puzzling and navigation. The combat is also pretty fun, and the game is not overly long either. If you're a fan of the genre, this is a definite buy. Also, it runs brilliantly on Steam Deck, which is where I spent most of my time playing this game. However, do know that if you are someone that likes to switch back and forth from deck to desktop, know that you are required to use Ubisoft's stupid cloud save system rather than Steam's. I cannot believe Ubisoft insists upon this. It's dumb and bad.   To add just a few points of criticism, I do think that most of the puzzling with those time powers are fairly easy along the critical path. Certainly the game has a lot of optional content both with side quests and straight up challenges that do introduce some added difficulty, but you will rarely be stumped along the main path. Also, the combat is fun, and the bosses are super cinematic and cool, but I do find the game fairly easy. Pretty much all of the bosses only took me a couple tries or so to get them down, where as Hollow Knight and Silksong bosses often ground me into pulp as I learned them.  Still, those are just some minor criticisms to an otherwise great game.

Story could've been better, main plot is kinda mid, but some twists are interesting and make me wonder why didn't they go more in certain direction ([spoiler] multiple timelines where prince fails, until you do "something" right and then you suceed [/spoiler]), but lore is plenty and takes some stuff from Persian mythology which is pretty cool as I cannot remember any other games which did something like that.  All and all pretty good metroidvania, plays great on Steam Deck and the Ubi launcher actually wasn't causing any troubles for me there, so I can only recommend this.

Un juego impresionante, la verdad, de los mejores metroidvania que jamás he jugado y soy muy fan del género. Tiene cierta dificultad pero no sádica como la de hollow knight (otro que adoro), tiene gráficos bonitos pero no espectaculares, priorizando el rendimiento en casi cualquier pc, lo que es genial, tiene plataformeo desafiante, tiene un sistema de combates tan currado que no parece propio del género, es casi de nivel juego de lucha (con "casi" estoy exagerando un poco, pero va claramente en esa dirección), y tiene una historia y narrativa genuinamente absorbentes.  Lo que más me ha entusiasmado es la creatividad. Algunos de los sistemas en este juego no los había visto nunca antes. No te da simplemente "vuelo" y ya como hacen en tantos metroidvania, o un "salta más alto", cada vez que te da algo así, trata de hacerlo de tal forma que no lo veas venir. Cada mecánica que es útil para moverse por el mapa también es útil para el combate. Hay luchas que puedes tener en este juego que no se pueden hacer en ningún otro juego existente, porque esas mecánicas lo cambian todo.  ...No es perfecto, ojo. Tiene sus puntos flacos. La historia y personajes son geniales pero deja muchos cabos sueltos cuando te lo pasas. Sospecho que algunos se resolverán en el dlc, que he comprado pero no he tenido tiempo de jugar. Pero no creo que los trate todos.  Este juego es como una sinfonía inacabada. Parece acabada para el oído casual, pero si prestas atención, lloras por el potencial perdido... ya que por supuesto, en un acto propio de Ubisoft, según sacan su primer juego genuinamente creativo y excelente en años, desmantelaron al equipo de desarrollo entero y clavaron la tapa del ataúd de Prince of Persia.   Es frustrante el mero hecho de que Ubisoft siga existiendo. Semejante incompetencia sólo puede seguir dominando los mercados en el triste mundo actual donde quienes pagan son los accionistas, no los jugadores. Este mundo que hemos creado donde en gaming, los gamers no importamos.  En fin, el juego es toda una oda a Prince of Persia y la corona perfecta al género Metroidvania. No sé si Metroid Dread me gusta más. Hollow knight, Metroid Dread y este juego, mi top metroidvanias. Y de todos ellos, este es el que más rápido me he pasado y el que más me ha divertido, auqnue no tenga tanta ambientación como hollow knight ni tanto suspense como dread.

A little over 30 hours played, with around 85% completion. I skipped the optional challenges under the Forge because they are really long and have no checkpoints. I also left a few treasures on the map: one puzzle that would have taken at least 20 minutes, and a couple of platforming sections that made me think “yeah… no thanks.”  There were also a few too many glitches. At one point I got softlocked and had to replay about half an hour of the Tower of Silence, which was pretty annoying.  That said, it’s a fantastic game. I’m genuinely surprised by how lukewarm the reception has been. For me, this might be the best Prince of Persia I’ve ever played, and easily the best Ubisoft game in at least 10 years.  Combat is not really the game’s strongest point, even though it is fairly deep and varied. There are three or four excellent boss fights and a few more that are definitely memorable, but outside of those, combat in the regular areas feels somewhat secondary. Maybe it changes at higher difficulties.  Where the game truly shines is exploration and platforming. Maybe it’s because, Hollow Knight aside, I have not played many Metroidvanias, but this really clicked with me. Tons of powers and abilities to unlock, and a huge map. I would not be surprised if it is larger than Silksong’s, even if it is clearly less dense.  Some platforming sections will raise your swearing counter quite a bit. As mentioned, I skipped a couple of them. Still, overall, I found the platforming genuinely fun and satisfying.  Highly recommended.
